target.title: fix(sessions): heal stale running rows on abort no-active-run and restart
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `sessions.abort` can return `status: "no-active-run"` while leaving a persisted session row stuck in `status: "running"`, causing lane-level message starvation.
- Solution: reconcile stale `running` rows on the no-active-run abort path and broaden restart recovery to include markerless stale running rows.
- What changed: added conservative stale-row healing in `sessions.abort`, expanded restart recovery selection, and added regression tests.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no protocol/API shape changes, no new config flags, no changes to normal active-run abort semantics.

## Motivation

Users can hit silent queue starvation: new messages enqueue behind a dead `running` lock and never execute until manual `/new` recovery. This PR closes that stale-state gap.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #59878
- Related #82433
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: stale persisted `running` session state survives `sessions.abort` no-active-run and startup recovery.
- Real environment tested: live OpenClaw gateway runtime on local machine + targeted test runtime.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. Seed a real session row to `status: "running"` with no active run controller.
  2. Call gateway RPC `sessions.abort` with that key.
  3. Read session row state before and after abort.
  4. Run regression tests and targeted lint.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):
  ```text
  AFTER-FIX proof before abort: {"status":"running","abortedLastRun":false}
  AFTER-FIX abort response: {"ok":true,"abortedRunId":null,"status":"no-active-run"}
  AFTER-FIX after abort: {"status":null,"abortedLastRun":false}
  ```
  Supplemental commands executed:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/sessions.abort-agent-scope.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/sessions.ts src/gateway/server-methods/sessions.abort-agent-scope.test.ts src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.ts src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts`
- Observed result after fix:
  - `sessions.abort` still returns `no-active-run` when no controller exists.
  - Stale persisted `running` row is healed (`status` cleared) instead of remaining stuck.
  - Restart recovery includes markerless stale `running` rows.
- What was not tested:
  - Multi-node production traffic under prolonged queue pressure.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged):
  ```json
  {"ok":true,"abortedRunId":null,"status":"no-active-run"}
  {"status":"running","abortedLastRun":false}
  ```

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: stale-state reconciliation depended on explicit abort/run lifecycle markers; if a run died without cleanup, `sessions.abort` could return `no-active-run` without mutating persisted row state.
- Missing detection / guardrail: no conservative fallback to heal `status: "running"` when no active run exists.
- Contributing context (if known): restart recovery required `abortedLastRun === true`, excluding markerless stale rows.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `src/gateway/server-methods/sessions.abort-agent-scope.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in:
  - `sessions.abort` returns `no-active-run` and stale persisted `running` row is healed.
  - Restart recovery includes stale `running` rows without pre-set `abortedLastRun` marker.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:
  - Directly exercises the two affected state-transition seams with minimal unrelated runtime noise.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Sessions stuck in stale `running` state are now automatically cleared on `sessions.abort` no-active-run and at startup recovery, reducing indefinite message queue stalls.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[sessions.abort -> no active run] -> [row stays running] -> [lane starves]

After:
[sessions.abort -> no active run] -> [stale running row healed] -> [lane proceeds]

Startup recovery:
Before: running + abortedLastRun!=true -> skipped
After:  running (markerless stale) -> marked + recovered
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS (Darwin x64)
- Runtime/container: OpenClaw gateway + Node.js test runtime
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Gateway server methods + agent recovery tests
- Relevant config (redacted): default local config; proof replay used isolated dev runtime state

### Steps

1. Seed stale `running` session row with no active run.
2. Invoke `sessions.abort` with that key.
3. Verify before/after row state.
4. Run regression tests and targeted lint.

### Expected

- No-active-run branch heals stale persisted `running` row.
- Restart recovery handles markerless stale `running` rows.

### Actual

- Matches expected; live proof and targeted tests passed.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing behavior before + healed behavior after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios:
  - no-active-run abort stale-row healing path
  - non-running row remains untouched
  - markerless stale running row recovered at restart
- Edge cases checked:
  - no-op when row not running
  - existing active-run abort behavior preserved
- What you did **not** verify:
  - full production-like multi-channel crash injection E2E

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: accidentally clearing a genuinely active session row.
  - Mitigation: reconciliation is guarded by no-active-run abort outcome and conservative startup recovery flow; active-run paths remain unchanged.

target.title: feat(whatsapp): support list reply actions
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: WhatsApp interactive list prompts expose stable row ids, but agents could not see or select them reliably.
- Why it matters: business and service flows often require a real WhatsApp `singleSelectReply.selectedRowId`, not a plain-text answer.
- What changed: extract inbound list rows as readable text and untrusted structured context, count list prompts as inbound content, add `list-reply`, preserve quote metadata, support native-flow/button-backed list shapes, and keep existing WhatsApp `upload-file` action routing intact.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no gateway protocol change, no auth or credential behavior change, and no config schema change.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #83217
- Related #73823
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: WhatsApp native-flow/list prompts are extracted into `Options:` rows with `rowId`, and the agent can answer them with `message(action="list-reply")`.

Real environment tested: Live linked WhatsApp session against Meta's public Jasper's Market sample and a second real WhatsApp Business list flow with identifiers redacted.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. Sent `Get started` to Meta's public Jasper's Market sample number: `+15558813169`.
   Public test link: https://api.whatsapp.com/send/?phone=15558813169&text=Get+started&type=phone_number&app_absent=0
2. Ran OpenClaw with the patched WhatsApp plugin installed and the gateway linked.
3. Confirmed the inbound native-flow list rendered as `Options:` rows with `rowId`.
4. Let the agent choose a visible option using `message(action="list-reply")`.

Equivalent OpenClaw trigger command:

```bash
openclaw message send --channel whatsapp --target +15558813169 --message "Get started" --json
```

Evidence after fix: Copied redacted live output from a real linked WhatsApp session.

Video proof using Meta's public Jasper's Market sample flow only:

Before:
https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/09d1206a-12ed-4c0e-a4ee-699c95fd7cdc

After:
https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/993dece2-bd9b-4286-bb99-e3ee9488fc39

The before video shows the public Jasper's Market WhatsApp list prompt being treated as plain text, leading to normal `message(action="send")` replies. The after video shows OpenClaw exposing `Options:` with `rowId`, the agent calling `message(action="list-reply")`, and the gateway sending a native WhatsApp list reply. No private business flow is included.

```text
Inbound prompt:
Welcome to Jasper's Market! What can we help you with today?
Options:
1. Shop online (rowId: reply-interactive-with-media)
2. Get recipe ideas (rowId: reply-media-card-carousel)
3. Current promo (rowId: reply-offer)

Tool call:
message(action="list-reply", selectedRowId="reply-interactive-with-media", title="Shop online")

Tool result:
{ "ok": true, "selectedRowId": "reply-interactive-with-media" }
```

Additional redacted live list-flow proof:

```text
Inbound prompt:
Options:
1. [redacted visible list option] (rowId: [redacted-row-id])
2. [redacted visible list option] (rowId: [redacted-row-id])

Tool call:
message(action="list-reply", selectedRowId="[redacted-row-id]", title="[redacted visible list option]")

Tool result:
{ "ok": true, "selectedRowId": "[redacted-row-id]" }
```

Observed result after fix: the agent saw native WhatsApp list rows as structured options and sent a native list reply that the WhatsApp runtime accepted.

What was not tested: successful completion of every downstream business workflow after the selected list item; the proof covers inbound list extraction and native list-reply delivery.

Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Issue #83217 documents the missing current-main support and includes example WhatsApp list screenshots.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/outbound/message-action-spec.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/action-runtime.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/channel-message-action.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/channel-actions.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/extract.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/send-api.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/send.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound.media.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-runtime-api-guardrails.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/quoted-message.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/whatsapp/src/channel-actions.test.ts src/agents/tools/message-tool.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/extract.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/send-api.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/action-runtime.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/channel-message-action.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/send.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound.media.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/quoted-message.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/whatsapp/src/channel-message-action.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/channel-actions.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/send.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-spec.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm check:changed`
- `pnpm build`

Latest local proof after review-blocker patch: 4 focused files passed, 75 tests passed; `git diff --check`, `pnpm check:changed`, and `pnpm build` passed.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

N/A. This is a new WhatsApp feature request rather than a regression in existing behavior.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/extract.test.ts`, `extensions/whatsapp/src/action-runtime.test.ts`, `extensions/whatsapp/src/channel-message-action.test.ts`, `extensions/whatsapp/src/channel-actions.test.ts`, `extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/send-api.test.ts`, `extensions/whatsapp/src/send.test.ts`, `extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound.media.test.ts`, `src/infra/outbound/message-action-spec.test.ts`, `src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-runtime-api-guardrails.test.ts`, `extensions/whatsapp/src/quoted-message.test.ts`.
- Scenario the test should lock in: direct and wrapped list prompt extraction, native-flow list extraction, structured context propagation, content gating, canonical message action schema, row id/title validation, outbound list/button reply payload shape, quote metadata preservation, upload-file routing preservation, plugin-owned aliases, and redacted outbound list-reply logs.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the behavior spans plugin extraction, action discovery/dispatch, and the Baileys send seam without needing a live WhatsApp account for deterministic payload proof.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): N/A before this change.
- If no new test is added, why not: New tests were added.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

WhatsApp now advertises and supports a native `list-reply` message action for inbound list prompts. Agents should pass `selectedRowId` and the visible row `title`.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
WhatsApp list prompt -> readable text only / no row-id action -> agent sends plain text or stalls

After:
WhatsApp list prompt -> readable options + untrusted structured context -> list-reply action -> WhatsApp singleSelectReply.selectedRowId
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? Yes
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? Yes
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: This adds a WhatsApp `list-reply` message action under the existing message tool. It is gated by the existing WhatsApp `actions.sendMessage` gate plus account/allowlist authorization, requires an explicit `selectedRowId` and visible `title`, treats inbound list context as untrusted, keeps target aliases plugin-owned, and avoids logging row titles, descriptions, or selected row ids.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: local developer environment (redacted)
- Runtime/container: Node 22, pnpm
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): WhatsApp plugin
- Relevant config (redacted): WhatsApp channel test configs and mocked active listener/send APIs

### Steps

1. Run the focused WhatsApp/action tests listed above.
2. Run `git diff --check`.
3. Run `pnpm check:changed`.
4. Run `pnpm build`.
5. For public live reproduction, message Jasper's Market at `+15558813169` with `Get started`, then verify the agent sees `Options:` rows and uses `message(action="list-reply")`.

### Expected

- Tests pass, changed checks pass, build passes, no whitespace errors are reported, and live list prompts expose selectable `rowId` values.

### Actual

target.title: feat(synology-chat): add configurable triggerWord to replace payload-based stripping
target.body:
## Problem

The channel currently strips `payload.trigger_word` (the field Synology
Chat populates on each webhook delivery) from the message text. This
field is not user-controlled and its value depends on the Synology Chat
configuration in unpredictable ways:

- When no trigger word is configured in Synology Chat, Synology
  populates `trigger_word` with the full message text for CJK input, and
  with the first word of the message for ASCII input. This means:
  - CJK messages are silently dropped (stripped text is empty → 204,
    no log entry, no agent delivery)
  - ASCII messages are delivered with the first word missing
- When a trigger word is configured, users expecting to send the trigger
  word alone (e.g. a one-word ping) get their message silently dropped

Both failures produce no log entry and no feedback to the user.

## Solution

Replace the implicit payload-based stripping with an explicit opt-in
config field: `channels.synology-chat.triggerWord`.

- **Default (unset)**: full message text is delivered unchanged.
  `payload.trigger_word` is ignored entirely.
- **When set**: the configured prefix is stripped from the start of the
  message, but only when the result is non-empty. A message consisting
  solely of the trigger word is delivered in full rather than dropped.

## Usage

```json
{
  "channels": {
    "synology-chat": {
      "triggerWord": "!bot"
    }
  }
}
```

Messages starting with `!bot ` are delivered with the prefix removed.
Messages that are exactly `!bot` are delivered as `!bot`. Messages with
no `!bot` prefix are delivered unchanged.

## Changes

- `types.ts`: add `triggerWord?: string` to config fields; `string | undefined` to resolved account
- `config-schema.ts`: add `z.string().optional()` validation
- `accounts.ts`: pass through `merged.triggerWord`
- `webhook-handler.ts`: use `account.triggerWord` instead of `payload.trigger_word`; add non-empty guard on stripped result
- `webhook-handler.test.ts`: update existing test; add cases for full-text preservation and no-config pass-through

## Real behavior proof

Tested on OpenClaw v2026.5.12, Ubuntu 22.04, live Synology Chat outgoing webhook.
No `triggerWord` configured.

Synology Chat's observed behavior for ASCII text when no trigger word is set in
Synology Chat:
- `text` field = full message text
- `trigger_word` field = first word of the message

For a single-word message this means `text == trigger_word`.

### Old code path (`payload.trigger_word`, before this PR)

```js
// sanitizeSynologyWebhookText — old
if (payload.trigger_word && cleanText.startsWith(payload.trigger_word)) {
  const stripped = cleanText.slice(payload.trigger_word.length).trim();
  if (stripped) cleanText = stripped;
}
```

- `text="ping"`, `trigger_word="ping"` → `stripped = ""`, guard fails, `cleanText = ""`
  → early `respondNoContent(res)` + `return { ok: false }` → **204, no log entry, no delivery**
- `text="hello world"`, `trigger_word="hello"` → `cleanText = "world"`
  → delivered with first word missing

### New code path (`account.triggerWord`, this PR)

With no `triggerWord` in config, `account.triggerWord` is `undefined`.
The stripping block is skipped entirely. Both payloads pass through unchanged.

```
curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" … \
  -d "token=<redacted>&user_id=99&username=testuser&text=ping&trigger_word=ping"
# → 204

curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" … \
  -d "token=<redacted>&user_id=99&username=testuser&text=hello+world&trigger_word=hello"
# → 204
```

Gateway log (both messages reached the agent):

```
[synology-chat] Message from testuser (99): ping
[synology-chat] Agent reply started for 99
[synology-chat] Message from testuser (99): hello world
[synology-chat] Agent reply started for 99
```

Note: the gateway always returns 204 to the outgoing webhook (including the silent-drop
path). The log entry `Message from … (99): <text>` is the proof of delivery — the drop
path produces **no such log line**.

target.title: feat(plugin-sdk): registerChatStreamRenderer for plugin-owned inline UI
target.body:
This PR adds a narrow SDK/Gateway seam for plugin-owned inline chat-stream UI without making plugins mutate core chat rendering directly. Reviewers can treat it as a descriptor/projection feature: plugins register chat-stream renderers, the Gateway projects ordered descriptors, and clients decide how to render them.

## Summary

This PR adds the missing OpenClaw plugin SDK seam for plugin-owned inline chat-stream UI. The important design boundary is that plugins declare metadata only; they do not ship rendering code into the sandbox. The host/Gateway projects typed descriptors, and compatible clients decide how to render surfaces such as inline plan cards, input-bar ownership, or input-toolbar chips.

- Problem: current Control UI descriptors cover static surfaces only (`session`, `tool`, `run`, `settings`), so plugins cannot express inline chat-stream UI without core rendering changes.
- Why it matters: plugin features like plan approval cards need a first-class, declarative host seam instead of bespoke core UI patches.
- What changed: adds `chat-message`, `chat-input-bar`, and `chat-input-toolbar-chip` descriptor surfaces, plus optional `priority` and chat-only `activeWhen` metadata, Gateway projection ordering, docs, and Swift protocol bindings.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no plugin-rendered JS/React crosses into core; the seam is metadata/projection only, and static descriptor slots remain backward compatible.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related electricsheephq/Smarter-Claw#78
- Related electricsheephq/Smarter-Claw#77
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## What's Added

| Surface | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `chat-message` | Plugin-owned widget rendered between chat messages, such as an inline plan-approval card. |
| `chat-input-bar` | Plugin-owned contribution to the chat input bar, such as owning the composer while approval is pending. |
| `chat-input-toolbar-chip` | Plugin-owned chip rendered in the chat input toolbar, such as an active mode indicator. |

Two optional descriptor fields are added:

- `priority?: number`: deterministic sort key among multiple descriptors on the same surface.
- `activeWhen?: { sessionExtensionNamespace: string; valuePath?: string[]; equals?: JsonValue }`: chat-stream-only predicate evaluated by compatible clients against the existing session-extension projection.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: plugin-authored chat-stream Control UI descriptors need to survive contract validation and appear in Gateway `plugins.uiDescriptors` output with deterministic ordering and attached plugin metadata.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-1`, branch `feat/plugin-sdk-chat-stream-renderer`, latest patch `50d2d25d265`, exercising plugin contract validation plus Gateway descriptor projection tests. This is OpenClaw contract/Gateway proof, not a browser-rendered consumer plugin run.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 pnpm test src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-host-hooks.test.ts
pnpm exec oxlint src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-host-hooks.ts src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-host-hooks.test.ts src/plugins/host-hooks.ts src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts --format default
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-host-hooks.ts src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-host-hooks.test.ts src/plugins/host-hooks.ts src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts docs/plugins/hooks.md docs/plugins/sdk-overview.md
git diff --check
```

- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```text
[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 5.41s
Contract shard: Test Files 1 passed, Tests 54 passed
Gateway shard: Test Files 1 passed, Tests 4 passed

oxlint: Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
oxfmt: All matched files use the correct format.
git diff --check: passed
```

Gateway projection test emits client-visible descriptor rows equivalent to:

```json
[
  { "pluginId": "m-plugin", "id": "negative", "surface": "chat-message", "priority": -5 },
  { "pluginId": "a-plugin", "id": "card-1", "surface": "chat-message", "priority": 10 },
  { "pluginId": "a-plugin", "id": "card-2", "surface": "chat-message", "priority": 10 },
  { "pluginId": "z-plugin", "id": "card-1", "surface": "chat-message", "priority": 50 },
  { "pluginId": "m-plugin", "id": "no-priority", "surface": "chat-input-toolbar-chip" }
]
```

- Observed result after fix: chat-stream descriptors validate, malformed `priority`/`activeWhen` values are rejected, `activeWhen` is rejected on static surfaces, `plugins.uiDescriptors` returns deterministic priority/plugin/id ordering, and docs now list the chat-stream surfaces in both hooks and SDK overview docs.
- What was not tested: a browser-rendered Smarter-Claw plan card. That consumer is intentionally blocked on this upstream SDK seam landing.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): ClawSweeper found `docs/plugins/sdk-overview.md` still listed only the four static surfaces; patch `50d2d25d265` updates that public overview.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

N/A. This is a new SDK/Gateway capability, not a regression in existing behavior.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-host-hooks.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: chat-stream descriptor validation, static-surface rejection for `activeWhen`, malformed metadata rejection, deterministic Gateway projection ordering.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the API seam is metadata validation/projection, so contract and Gateway handler tests cover the core behavior without a consumer UI.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): none before this PR.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A, tests are included.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Plugin authors gain declarative chat-stream Control UI descriptor surfaces. Existing clients/plugins that do not understand the new surfaces can ignore them; existing static descriptor behavior remains unchanged.

## Diagram (if applicable)

<!-- reviewer-map -->

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  plugin["Plugin registers chat-stream renderer"] --> host["Plugin host descriptor registry"]
  host --> gateway["Gateway projection"]
  gateway --> client["Control UI / Swift client descriptor"]
  client --> render["Plugin-owned inline chat UI"]
  plugin -. "no direct core chat mutation" .-> render
```

```text
[Plugin registers descriptor metadata]
  -> [Plugin registry validates surface, priority, activeWhen]
  -> [Gateway plugins.uiDescriptors projects sorted rows]
  -> [Compatible client renders chat-stream UI]

No plugin rendering code crosses the sandbox boundary.
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`): No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`): No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A. The PR adds descriptor metadata and projection only.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS local development host
- Runtime/container: local Lexar-backed OpenClaw checkout
- Model/provider: not model-provider dependent
- Integration/channel (if any): plugin SDK contract and Gateway `plugins.uiDescriptors` handler
- Relevant config (redacted): none

### Steps

1. Run contract and Gateway descriptor tests.
2. Run targeted lint and formatter checks.
3. Verify docs mention all chat-stream descriptor surfaces.

### Expected

- Descriptor validation and Gateway projection tests pass.
- SDK docs list chat-stream surfaces.
- No lint/format failures.

### Actual

- 58 focused tests passed.
- Targeted lint, formatter, and whitespace checks passed.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: contract validation, malformed metadata rejection, static-surface `activeWhen` rejection, Gateway projection order, plugin metadata attachment, docs wording.
- Edge cases checked: negative priority, missing priority, plugin/id tie-breakers, docs `chat-input-toolbar-chip` wording.
- What you did **not** verify: rendered browser consumer UI and full GitHub CI matrix.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

ClawSweeper P3 addressed by `50d2d25d265`: update the SDK overview surface list. Prior lint/doc cleanup is included in remote commits `3df8c394d72`, `34e7ba5ec12`, and `4f6f0f71c58`.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`): Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`): No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: this is a new public SDK/Gateway seam and needs maintainer API-shape approval.
  - Mitigation: the seam is declarative metadata only, rejects malformed descriptors before projection, and preserves client-owned rendering.

target.title: feat #94529: auto-derive session title in sidebar
target.body:
## Summary

Control UI session lists now request derived titles from the gateway and fall back to them when a session has no user-set label or display name. This makes the sidebar and chat session picker distinguishable after opening multiple sessions.

## Root Cause

`sessions.list` already supports `includeDerivedTitles`, but the Control UI frontend never requested it, so the sidebar showed generic labels such as "New Session" for fresh sessions. The chat session picker also received derived titles but did not render them when no `label`/`displayName` was present.

## Changes

- `ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.ts`: include `includeDerivedTitles: true` in sidebar session list requests.
- `ui/src/ui/chat/session-controls.ts`: include `includeDerivedTitles: true` in chat session picker requests; `resolveSessionScopedOptionLabel()` now treats a non-key `derivedTitle` like `label` / `displayName` and calls `resolveSessionDisplayName()` instead of falling back to the parsed key rest.
- `ui/src/ui/types.ts`: add `derivedTitle?: string` to `GatewaySessionRow`.
- `ui/src/ui/session-display.ts`: use `derivedTitle` as a fallback after explicit `label` and `displayName`.

## Regression Test Plan

- Updated `ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts` to assert `includeDerivedTitles: true` in expected `sessions.list` params.
- Updated `ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts` for chat session picker params and added derived-title-only picker render coverage.
- Added `resolveSessionDisplayName` tests in `ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts` covering `derivedTitle` fallback and precedence over explicit labels.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Control UI sidebar and chat session picker showed generic labels for fresh sessions instead of derived titles.

Real environment tested: Linux development checkout running the Control UI test harness.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
pnpm test ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts --run
```

Evidence after fix:
```
✓ ui/ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts (78 tests)
✓ ui/ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts (77 tests)
✓ unit-ui/ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts (111 tests)

Test Files 3 passed (3)
Tests 266 passed (266)
```

These tests verify that the frontend now requests `includeDerivedTitles: true`, that `derivedTitle` is used as a fallback display name, and that the chat session picker renders `derivedTitle` for rows that have no explicit `label`/`displayName`.

Observed result after fix: `sessions.list` calls from the sidebar and chat session picker include `includeDerivedTitles: true`; `resolveSessionDisplayName` returns the derived title when no explicit label/displayName exists; `resolveSessionScopedOptionLabel` no longer falls back to the parsed key rest for derived-title-only rows.

What was not tested: A live browser screenshot of the Control UI sidebar rendering multiple sessions with distinct derived titles (requires a running gateway and browser). Requesting maintainer `proof:` override for the missing live UI screenshot.

Closes #94529.

target.title: fix(agents): keep apostrophes in slash-command prompt-template arguments
target.body:
## Summary

- `parseCommandArgs` (`packages/agent-core/src/harness/prompt-template-arguments.ts`) expands `$ARGUMENTS` / `$@` / `$1..$N` in user prompt templates and custom slash commands. It does shell-style quote parsing with **no escape and no unbalanced-quote recovery**, so a lone `'` (an ordinary apostrophe in English prose: `user's`, `it's`, `don't`) opens a quote that runs to the next apostrophe (or end of string). The apostrophe is dropped and the following whitespace is swallowed into one token. The corrupted tokens then feed `$ARGUMENTS` (`args.join(" ")`) and the positional placeholders, so the model **silently receives a mangled instruction** with no error or warning.
  - `/cmd don't it's broken now` + a `[$1][$2]` template collapses to `ARG1=[dont its] ARG2=[broken]`: both apostrophes dropped, placeholders shifted.
- Reach: shipped and default-on. `AgentSession.prompt()` expands templates by default (`expandPromptTemplates ?? true`) and the **expanded text becomes the user message sent to the model**. Templates are real user `.md` files loaded by `loadPromptTemplates` from `<agentDir>/prompts`, `<cwd>/.openclaw/prompts`, and explicit prompt paths.
- Fix: single-quote handling is now an explicit shell-style grammar with no hardcoded contraction word list. A word-attached apostrophe is a delimiter only when it opens a concatenation that closes at a later trailing apostrophe (`foo'bar baz'`), and it yields to any nearer apostrophe that owns such a closer, so a leading prose word keeps its apostrophe (`y'all foo'bar baz'`, `don't 'quoted text' next`). A standalone or assignment-attached apostrophe prefers a later whitespace-spanning quote (`'echo one two'`, `foo='bar baz'`) and otherwise closes at the first apostrophe that is not an English contraction suffix, so shell-style concatenation still works before suffix text (`'foo'bar`, `'foo bar'baz` -> `foo barbaz`) while an unmatched leading quote before a contraction stays literal (`'review don't fail`). An apostrophe with no matching closer stays literal. Irregular contractions are recognized structurally rather than by an enumerated list, so `y'all`, `ma'am`, `o'clock`, `ne'er`, `cap'n`, and `y'know` are all preserved; only a small fixed set of English contraction suffixes (`d`, `ll`, `m`, `re`, `s`, `t`, `ve`) is consulted to tell a contraction apostrophe from a closing quote that precedes suffix text. Double-quote tokenization is unchanged from current main, including the unmatched case (`say "hello world` -> `["say", "hello world"]`). This replaces the prior revision's hardcoded `proseContractionWords` set while preserving all other shipped quote behavior.
- One canonical change in `parseCommandArgs`; both consumers (`expandPromptTemplate` in `src/agents/sessions/prompt-templates.ts` and `formatPromptTemplateInvocation` in `packages/agent-core`) benefit with no API change. The separate `src/auto-reply/commands-registry.ts` `parseCommandArgs` (native channel commands) is a different function and is left untouched.

## Tests

- `packages/agent-core/src/harness/prompt-templates.test.ts`: added cases covering apostrophes preserved in parsed tokens and through `$ARGUMENTS` / positional placeholders; multiple contractions; shell-style quoted spans embedded in a token (`--title="two words"`, `foo='bar baz'`); bare embedded single-quote concatenation (`foo'bar baz'`); a multi-word quoted span that closes before suffix text (`'foo bar'baz` -> `foo barbaz`, preserving current main); an unmatched double quote that groups to the end of input (`say "hello world`, `"unterminated`, `a"b`); a plural possessive next to a later quoted span (`fix users' 'quoted text'`); apostrophes after non-ASCII word characters (`José's don't fail`); an unmatched leading quote that must not close on a later contraction (`'review don't fail`); a contraction inside a balanced quoted phrase (`'it's a test'`); a contraction immediately before a later standalone single-quoted span (`don't 'quoted text' next`); irregular contractions before a later attached single-quote span (`y'all`, `ma'am`, `o'clock`); and a generality case proving any prose contraction is preserved without a fixed word list (`ne'er`, `cap'n`, `y'know`, `fo'c'sle`, plus closer-side `'y'all here'`). The existing quoted/multiline and placeholder-safety cases are unchanged and green (24/24).
- `src/agents/sessions/prompt-templates.test.ts` (`expandPromptTemplate`) unchanged and green.
- Touched suites run locally with the repo runner (`prompt-templates.test.ts` 24/24); tsgo core, type-aware oxlint, and oxfmt clean on the touched files.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: a user-authored custom slash command / prompt template invoked with ordinary prose containing an apostrophe silently lost the apostrophe (and, for positional `$N` templates, collapsed arguments and shifted later placeholders), so the model received a corrupted instruction. Covers prose contractions of every shape including irregular ones the parser does not enumerate (`y'all`, `o'clock`, `ne'er`, `cap'n`, `y'know`) and a contraction inside a balanced span (`'y'all here'`). Shipped quote behavior is preserved and verified to stay byte-identical to current main: shell-style concatenation including a multi-word span before suffix text (`'foo bar'baz` -> `foo barbaz`) and unmatched double quotes (`say "hello world`).
- Real environment tested: Linux, Node 22.19, exercising the actual production functions `loadPromptTemplates` + `expandPromptTemplate` (the same expansion path `AgentSession.prompt()` uses to build the outbound user message) over a real on-disk prompt directory (`<agentDir>/prompts/*.md`).
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: wrote `<tmp>/prompts/cmd.md` = `ARG1=[$1] ARG2=[$2] ARG3=[$3] ARG4=[$4]`; loaded it via `loadPromptTemplates({ cwd, agentDir, promptPaths, includeDefaults: false })`; called `expandPromptTemplate("/cmd <input>", templates)` for each input below; ran the script over the **current PR head** (`d7be396cb6`) and, for the before column, over the `origin/main` parser source (`substituteArgs` is identical, so only `parseCommandArgs` differs) with the rest of the path identical.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): copied live output from running the real `expandPromptTemplate` over the on-disk template on the current PR head `d7be396cb6`:


target.title: fix(cli): reject finite-but-unsafe byte sizes
target.body:
## Summary

`parseByteSize` (`src/cli/parse-bytes.ts`) computed `Math.round(value * multiplier)` and guarded only with `if (!Number.isFinite(bytes))`. For inputs above `Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER` the result is **finite but not a safe integer**, so the guard passed and the function silently returned a precision-corrupted value — e.g. `parseByteSize("9007199254740993")` returned `9007199254740992` (a different number than typed) and `parseByteSize("9000000tb")` returned `9895604649984000000`.

Its direct sibling `parseDurationMs` (`src/cli/parse-duration.ts`) was changed in commit `35cdd40182` ("fix(cli): reject unsafe duration values") from the identical `Number.isFinite` check to a `roundSafeDurationMs` helper that throws on `!Number.isSafeInteger`. `parse-bytes.ts` is the twin left on the old, now-known-buggy contract. `parseByteSize` is consumed on config-validation/runtime paths (`config/zod-schema.ts` runLog.maxBytes, session maxDiskBytes/highWaterBytes, `cron/run-log.ts`).

### Changes
- `parse-bytes.ts` — add `roundSafeByteSize(raw, value)` that throws via the existing `invalidByteSize` when the rounded result is not a safe integer, mirroring `roundSafeDurationMs`. `Number.isSafeInteger` also covers the non-finite case the old guard handled.
- `cli-utils.test.ts` — finite-but-unsafe cases.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: byte sizes above `Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER` were accepted and returned precision-corrupted instead of being rejected. After the fix they throw; valid sizes are unchanged.

Real environment tested: Node 22.22.1, macOS, no network/model. Vitest exercises the real exported `parseByteSize`. BEFORE is `origin/main`'s file (swapped via `git show`).

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/cli-utils.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix:
```
parseByteSize("9007199254740993")  BEFORE: 9007199254740992 (corrupted)   AFTER: throws "Invalid byte size"
parseByteSize("9000000tb")         BEFORE: 9895604649984000000 (corrupted) AFTER: throws "Invalid byte size"
parseByteSize("10mb")              BOTH: 10485760  (valid, unchanged)
```

Observed result after fix: out-of-safe-range sizes are rejected; all existing valid/invalid cases are unchanged. The new tests fail on `origin/main` and pass after.

What was not tested: end-to-end config validation (the test drives the real exported `parseByteSize` those schemas call).

## Additional checks
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/cli-utils.test.ts` passes; fail-before verified by swapping in `origin/main`'s file. `oxlint --tsconfig` clean.

target.title: feat(plugins): add Control UI entry points
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Plugins can expose gateway-authenticated HTTP routes, but there was no generic way for a plugin to register a first-class Control UI navigation entry.
- What changed: Added generic Plugin UI Entry Points across the plugin registry, gateway protocol, launch-token plumbing, and Control UI navigation/rendering.
- Security follow-up: Plugin UI entry registration, launch/session auth, and iframe bridge proxying now validate canonical decoded plugin roots, so encoded dot-segment paths cannot let one plugin UI session authorize sibling plugin routes.
- Scope boundary: This PR does not add the Session Search plugin or any plugin-specific UI. The proof plugin lives separately on [`Ittiz/openclaw@forge/session-search-plugin`](https://github.com/Ittiz/openclaw/tree/forge/session-search-plugin).

## What Problem This Solves

Plugins can expose gateway-authenticated HTTP routes, but before this PR they had no generic, first-class way to place a Control UI navigation entry that launches a plugin-owned UI through the gateway authorization boundary. This PR adds that contract while keeping plugin entry paths and iframe bridge requests confined to the owning plugin root.

## Working Example Plugin

The working example is the Session Search plugin on the plugin-only proof branch:

- Branch: [`Ittiz/openclaw@forge/session-search-plugin`](https://github.com/Ittiz/openclaw/tree/forge/session-search-plugin)
- README: [`README.md`](https://github.com/Ittiz/openclaw/blob/forge/session-search-plugin/README.md)
- Entry point registration: [`index.ts`](https://github.com/Ittiz/openclaw/blob/forge/session-search-plugin/index.ts)
- Manifest: [`openclaw.plugin.json`](https://github.com/Ittiz/openclaw/blob/forge/session-search-plugin/openclaw.plugin.json)

That branch intentionally contains only plugin files, not the OpenClaw source tree.

## Evidence

### Real behavior proof

- Current head tested: `b8e35876a18a36390c867080e475d53f26fb4e15`.
- Behavior addressed: Plugin UI entry points can be listed/launched/rendered; sandboxed opaque-origin iframes receive the MessagePort bridge; decoded/canonicalized plugin paths remain confined to the owning plugin root; new entry-point response schemas are exported through the public protocol barrel.
- Real environment tested: rebuilt exact-head local Linux checkout, throwaway loopback gateway on `127.0.0.1:19888`, temporary plugin loaded through `plugins.load.paths`, Control UI driven in a real headless Chromium browser, plus direct HTTP route checks.

Commands run after the current fix:

```bash
git diff --check
node scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs --check
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/navigation.browser.test.ts --reporter=verbose
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-host-hooks.test.ts src/gateway/server.plugin-http-auth.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts ui/src/ui/navigation.test.ts --reporter=verbose
OPENCLAW_BUILD_ALL_NO_PNPM=1 node scripts/build-all.mjs ciArtifacts
node .artifacts/pr80388-live-proof/run-proof.mjs
```

Live browser/plugin-route evidence from the throwaway exact-head gateway:

```json
{
  "head": "OpenClaw 2026.6.8 (726f348)",
  "controlUiUrl": "http://127.0.0.1:19888/overview#token=REDACTED",
  "pluginEntryVisible": true,
  "iframeMarker": "PR80388_PROOF_PLUGIN_IFRAME_READY",
  "bridgeText": "BRIDGE_STATUS: 200 {\"ok\":true,\"marker\":\"PR80388_PROOF_PLUGIN_API_READY\",\"pluginId\":\"proof-entry\"}",
  "directUnauthStatus": 401,
  "directAuthStatus": 200,
  "directAuthBody": "{\"ok\":true,\"marker\":\"PR80388_PROOF_PLUGIN_API_READY\",\"pluginId\":\"proof-entry\"}"
}
```

Focused test evidence:

```text
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/navigation.browser.test.ts --reporter=verbose
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  17 passed (17)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 9.24s

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-host-hooks.test.ts src/gateway/server.plugin-http-auth.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts ui/src/ui/navigation.test.ts --reporter=verbose
Test Files  4 passed (4)
Tests  68 passed (68)
[test] passed 3 Vitest shards in 68.19s

$ node scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs --check
package-exported forbidden subpaths: 0

$ OPENCLAW_BUILD_ALL_NO_PNPM=1 node scripts/build-all.mjs ciArtifacts
[build-all] phase timings: total 175.6s
```

Specific regression locked in:

- `ui/src/ui/navigation.browser.test.ts` verifies sandboxed in-app plugin requests are proxied only for plugin-owned paths and stale iframe bridge ports cannot service current-frame requests.
- `src/gateway/server.plugin-http-auth.test.ts` rejects Plugin UI Entry Point sessions when decoded path candidates leave the plugin root.
- `src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts` rejects encoded dot-segment sibling escapes at entry-point registration.
- `ui/src/ui/navigation.test.ts` rejects external, contextual, cross-plugin, and encoded sibling paths.

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? Yes. Plugins can expose Control UI entry points.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? Yes. Entry-point launch uses short-lived Plugin UI launch/session tokens.
- Data access scope changed? Yes. Entry points declare required operator scopes.
- Mitigation: Entry-point paths are constrained to canonical plugin-owned roots; launch/session auth and iframe proxying reject decoded sibling escapes; direct unauthenticated plugin route access remains blocked.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes.
- Config/env changes? No.
- Migration needed? No.

target.title: fix: clear cross-run messaging-tool sent-text state after every compaction
target.body:
## Problem

When a run completes and the session compacts, the arrays tracking texts sent via messaging tools (`message(action=send)`, `sessions_send`, etc.) were only cleared on compaction retries (`willRetry=true`). On normal compaction (`willRetry=false`), stale entries survived into the next run.

`isMessagingToolDuplicateNormalized()` compares the final reply text against these stale entries using substring matching (`normalized.includes(normalizedSent)`). If the new run's text happens to share a normalized substring with any entry from the previous run, the block reply is silently dropped with:

> Skipping message_end block reply - already sent via messaging tool

## Fix

Clear `messagingToolSentTexts`, `messagingToolSentTextsNormalized`, `messagingToolSentTargets`, and `messagingToolSentMediaUrls` unconditionally in the `compaction_end` handler — before the `willRetry` branch — so the next run always starts with a clean slate.

## Verification

- Relevant tests pass (6/6) in `pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.compaction.test.ts` (updated fixture)
- No logic change to `emitBlockChunk`, `handleMessageEnd`, or the dedup function itself — change is purely about state lifecycle between runs

target.title: fix: add resilient fallback policy for user model overrides
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: session/user model overrides always disabled configured fallback chains.
- Why it matters: operators could configure provider fallback behavior, but a user-selected model had no fallback safety net during transient provider/model failures.
- What changed: adds opt-in `userOverrideFallbackPolicy: "resilient"` on `agents.defaults.model` and per-agent chat model config, with schema/types/help/labels/docs and fallback-resolution tests.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): default behavior remains `strict`; auto-selected model overrides and configured agent primaries keep their existing fallback behavior; non-chat model config schemas remain unchanged.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes N/A
- Related N/A
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: a user/session model override can now inherit the configured fallback chain when the new policy is explicitly set to `resilient`, while the default `strict` behavior still returns no fallbacks.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on pop-os, Node v25.8.1, branch `fix/user-override-fallback-policy`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  ```bash
  pnpm exec tsx -e 'import { resolveEffectiveModelFallbacks } from "./src/agents/agent-scope.ts"; const cfg={agents:{defaults:{model:{primary:"openai/gpt-5.4",fallbacks:["anthropic/claude-haiku-3-5","google/gemini-2.5-flash"],userOverrideFallbackPolicy:"resilient"}},list:[{id:"linus"}]}}; console.log("user override resilient =>", JSON.stringify(resolveEffectiveModelFallbacks({cfg,agentId:"linus",hasSessionModelOverride:true,modelOverrideSource:"user"}))); const strict={agents:{defaults:{model:{primary:"openai/gpt-5.4",fallbacks:["anthropic/claude-haiku-3-5"]}},list:[{id:"linus"}]}}; console.log("user override strict =>", JSON.stringify(resolveEffectiveModelFallbacks({cfg:strict,agentId:"linus",hasSessionModelOverride:true,modelOverrideSource:"user"})));'
  ```
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):
  ```text
  user override resilient => ["anthropic/claude-haiku-3-5","google/gemini-2.5-flash"]
  user override strict => []
  ```
- Observed result after fix: resilient user overrides receive the configured fallback list; strict user overrides receive an empty fallback list.
- What was not tested: live provider failover against real external model APIs, because that would require changing a running gateway config. The changed resolver and config schema were tested locally.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): covered by the existing and new regression tests: the pre-change resolver returned `[]` for user overrides whenever `hasSessionModelOverride` was true.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

For bug fixes or regressions, explain why this happened, not just what changed. Otherwise write `N/A`. If the cause is unclear, write `Unknown`.

- Root cause: `resolveEffectiveModelFallbacks` treated every non-auto session model override as authoritative and returned an empty fallback list without a config escape hatch.
- Missing detection / guardrail: no regression coverage for user-selected models that should intentionally keep operator-configured fallback chains.
- Contributing context (if known): auto model overrides and user/session overrides were represented in the same resolver path, but operators need separate fallback policy for user choices.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

For bug fixes or regressions, name the smallest reliable test coverage that should catch this. Otherwise write `N/A`.

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/agents/agent-scope.test.ts`, `src/config/zod-schema.agent-defaults.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: user model overrides remain strict by default, can opt into resilient fallback inheritance, and per-agent policy overrides defaults.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the behavior is selected in the config schema and resolved in the agent-scope fallback helper before provider execution.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): N/A
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Users can set `userOverrideFallbackPolicy: "resilient"` under `agents.defaults.model` or an individual agent's chat `model` block. The default is still `strict`.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[user override] -> [fallbacks disabled]

After:
[user override] -> [strict policy] -> [fallbacks disabled]
[user override] -> [resilient policy] -> [configured fallbacks preserved]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: pop-os Linux
- Runtime/container: local Node v25.8.1 / pnpm
- Model/provider: N/A, pure config + fallback resolver change
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A
- Relevant config (redacted): `agents.defaults.model.userOverrideFallbackPolicy: "resilient"`

### Steps

1. Configure fallback models under `agents.defaults.model.fallbacks`.
2. Set `agents.defaults.model.userOverrideFallbackPolicy: "resilient"`.
3. Resolve fallbacks for a session/user model override.

### Expected

- Resilient user overrides inherit configured fallback models.
- Strict/default user overrides keep fallback models disabled.

### Actual

- Matches expected in the local resolver smoke and targeted tests.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Verification commands run:

```text
codex review --base origin/main
pnpm check:changed
pnpm test src/agents/agent-scope.test.ts src/config/zod-schema.agent-defaults.test.ts
pnpm config:docs:check && pnpm config:schema:check
git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
```

`codex review --base origin/main` result:

```text
The change consistently adds the new user override fallback policy across config types, schema validation, labels/help, docs, and fallback resolution. I did not find a discrete correctness issue in the modified paths.
```

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: strict default, resilient defaults, per-agent policy override, explicit fallback override behavior, config schema acceptance/rejection, generated config docs/schema consistency.
- Edge cases checked: explicit empty fallback list still disables fallback inheritance; non-chat model schemas still reject the chat-only policy field.
- What you did **not** verify: live external provider outage failover through a running gateway.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) Yes, optional new config field
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: set `userOverrideFallbackPolicy: "resilient"` only when user-selected models should keep configured fallback chains.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: operators may expect resilient behavior after adding fallbacks but forget the new policy.
  - Mitigation: docs and schema help call out the strict default and the explicit opt-in field.

target.title: feat: add WhatsApp read-only mode
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a true read-only mode for WhatsApp channels so OpenClaw can ingest and analyze inbound WhatsApp messages without ever sending outbound WhatsApp traffic.

Fixes openclaw/openclaw#71166.

## Problem

WhatsApp could emit automatic outbound messages during inbound handling, including access-not-configured notices, pairing prompts, replies, read receipts, reactions, polls, typing indicators, and other transport-level sends.

That made it unsafe to attach WhatsApp in observation-only or production-adjacent setups, because there was no hard configuration-level guarantee that OpenClaw would stay silent.

## Fix

This PR adds `readOnly: true` support for WhatsApp at both channel and per-account config scopes, then enforces it inside the WhatsApp transport as a hard outbound gate.

When enabled, WhatsApp still receives and processes inbound messages, but outbound actions are dropped before they can leave the system.

Covered outbound paths include:

- text and media sends
- polls
- reactions
- typing/composing indicators
- inbound reply/send API closures
- pairing replies
- read receipts
- connect presence

## Testing

- `pnpm tsgo:extensions --pretty false`
- Targeted WhatsApp tests: 4 files, 48 tests

## Notes

This is intentionally enforced at the transport layer so accidental replies from agents, default system notices, or edge-case error handling cannot bypass read-only mode.

target.title: feat: add tools.fs.roots — per-agent filesystem roots with access modes
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `tools.fs.workspaceOnly` is binary, so operators cannot safely allow agents to read or write a specific host path outside the workspace without opening broader filesystem access.
- Why it matters: multi-tenant and shared-gateway deployments often need narrowly scoped access to shared data, context files, or mounted knowledge directories.
- What changed: added `tools.fs.roots`, an explicit allowlist of filesystem roots with per-root `kind` (`dir`/`file`) and `access` (`ro`/`rw`), wired through host-mode FS tools, `apply_patch`, local media loading, outbound local-media/reply-delivery paths, and prompt-image auto-load.
- Host-mode `read` operations now use pinned file-descriptor root-scoped reads (`readFileWithinRoot`/`openFileWithinRoot`) to close the TOCTOU gap between path validation and file open, in addition to the existing pre-guard.
- Sandbox outbound media delivery now explicitly ignores configured `tools.fs.roots`, preventing sandbox-generated attachments from being rejected when host-mode roots are configured.
- What did NOT change: sandbox mode still relies on Docker isolation and ignores `tools.fs.roots`; `exec` sandboxing is unchanged; `tools.allow` / `tools.deny` behavior is unchanged.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause / Regression History (if applicable)

- Root cause: existing FS policy exposed only two modes for host paths: workspace-only or unrestricted.
- Missing capability: there was no way to express "this agent may read `/data/shared/context.md` but may not read the rest of the host".
- Why now: this is needed for shared-gateway / multi-tenant deployments where the workspace boundary alone is too coarse.
- If unknown, what was ruled out: sandbox mode stays out of scope because sandbox tools resolve container paths, not host paths, and partial host-path enforcement there would create an inconsistent security model.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should catch this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target tests/files:
  - `src/agents/tool-fs-policy.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/pi-tools.multi-root-guard.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/pi-tools.sandbox-mounted-paths.workspace-only.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/pi-tools.workspace-only-false.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/apply-patch.test.ts`
  - `src/media/local-roots.test.ts`
  - `src/media/load-options.test.ts`
  - `src/media/local-media-access.test.ts`
  - `src/auto-reply/reply/reply-media-paths.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/images.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/tools/image-tool.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/tools/pdf-tool.test.ts`
  - `src/infra/outbound/deliver.test.ts`
  - `src/infra/fs-safe.test.ts`
  - `src/infra/fs-pinned-write-helper.spawn-error.test.ts`
  - `src/infra/fs-safe.unlink-helper-error.test.ts`
  - `extensions/googlechat/src/actions.test.ts`
  - `extensions/googlechat/src/channel.test.ts`
  - `extensions/feishu/src/media.test.ts`
  - `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/reply-delivery.test.ts`
- Scenarios locked in:
  - lexical dir/file root matching and ro/rw enforcement
  - canonical-root precedence over alias roots for host-mode mutation paths
  - unlink/delete behavior preserving symlink-entry semantics while still respecting stricter canonical roots
  - roots precedence over `workspaceOnly` for host-mode FS access
  - sandbox-mode fallback still honoring `workspaceOnly`
  - rooted local image/PDF/reference-image loads outside the workspace
  - rooted outbound local-media and reply-delivery paths preserving file-vs-dir root semantics
  - rooted prompt-image auto-load outside the workspace
  - host-mode reads use pinned-FD root-scoped operations (TOCTOU-safe)
  - sandboxed outbound media delivery ignores configured host roots
  - rooted delete helper gracefully falls back when pinned write helper spawn fails
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the risky surfaces here are policy resolution, host-path mutation, and media path propagation, so targeted seam tests catch the actual failure modes without requiring a full live deployment.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New config surface: `tools.fs.roots` on global `tools.fs` and per-agent `tools.fs`.
- Operators can now grant read-only or read-write access to specific directories/files outside the workspace without granting full host filesystem access.
- Host-mode `read` / `write` / `edit` / `apply_patch` now respect those explicit roots.
- Local media loading, outbound local-media/reply-delivery flows, and prompt-image auto-load now preserve and enforce rooted file/dir access semantics.
- When both `roots` and `workspaceOnly` are set, `roots` takes precedence for host-mode FS access and a warning is logged.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:
  - This adds a new operator-controlled path restriction model for host-mode FS access. Risk is policy misunderstanding or incomplete enforcement across FS-adjacent surfaces. Mitigation: all configured roots are validated as absolute paths; enforcement is alias-safe; canonical root precedence is preserved for host-mode mutation paths; host-mode `read`/`write`/`edit`/`apply_patch`, local media inputs, outbound local-media/reply-delivery, and prompt-image auto-load are covered; host-mode reads use pinned-FD root-scoped operations to close TOCTOU gaps; sandbox mode explicitly ignores roots to avoid host/container path confusion.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS 15 / darwin 25.3.0
- Runtime/container: local repo checkout
- Model/provider: N/A for core FS policy; targeted local media tests use mocked image/PDF/image-generation providers
- Integration/channel (if any): host-mode FS tools + local media loading + outbound local-media reply flows
- Relevant config (redacted):

```json5
{
  "tools": {
    "fs": {
      "roots": [
        { "path": "/opt/app/workspaces/agent-1", "kind": "dir", "access": "rw" },
        { "path": "/data/finance/invoices", "kind": "dir", "access": "ro" },
        { "path": "/data/finance/context.md", "kind": "file", "access": "ro" }
      ]
    }
  }
}
```

### Steps

1. Configure an agent with `tools.fs.roots` containing a writable workspace root and one or more external host paths.
2. Attempt `read`/`write`/`edit`/`apply_patch` and local media access both inside and outside those roots.
3. Attempt outbound local-media / reply-delivery flows with both dir roots and exact file roots.
4. Attempt prompt-image auto-load from a rooted external image path.
5. Attempt the same config in sandbox mode.

### Expected

- Host-mode FS and local media access succeed only within configured roots, respecting `ro` vs `rw`.
- Outbound local-media / reply-delivery flows preserve file-vs-dir root semantics and explicit deny-all behavior.
- Prompt-image auto-load accepts explicitly rooted host paths.
- Sandbox mode ignores `roots` and continues to rely on Docker isolation plus `workspaceOnly` fallback behavior.

### Actual

- Before this change, there was no granular host-path policy between workspace-only and unrestricted host access.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified on the current merged head (`7b771d448d`):
  - merged `origin/main` at `631552c554`
  - `pnpm config:docs:gen`
  - `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:gen`
  - `pnpm check`
  - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 pnpm test src/agents/pi-tools.workspace-only-false.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.memory-flush-forwarding.test.ts`
  - `pnpm test src/media/read-capability.test.ts`
  - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 pnpm test src/media/read-capability.test.ts src/media/local-roots.test.ts`
  - `pnpm test extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/inbound-dispatch.test.ts`
  - `pnpm test src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts -t "sends transcriptPrompt visibly and queues runtime context as hidden custom context"`
  - `pnpm test src/agents/pi-tools.sandbox-mounted-paths.workspace-only.test.ts -t "ignores host roots for sandboxed read/write/edit tools"`
- Additional focused regressions were exercised during review for:
  - fs-root resolution / precedence
  - alias-safe host mutations and unlink/delete behavior
  - media root propagation and explicit deny-all handling
  - prompt-image and local-media rooted access
  - outbound local-media / reply-delivery adapters
  - pinned-FD host reads (TOCTOU closure)
  - sandbox outbound media ignoring configured roots
- What you did **not** verify on `7b771d448d`:
  - full local `pnpm test`
  - `pnpm build`
  - live end-to-end multi-tenant deployment behavior beyond local seam tests
  - `pnpm check:changed` did not complete locally: it passed type/lint/guard phases and 2,450 tests before a local Vitest gateway worker hit `ERR_WORKER_OUT_OF_MEMORY`

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: remove `tools.fs.roots` from config or revert this PR.
- Files/config to restore: `src/agents/pi-tools.ts`, `src/agents/tool-fs-policy.ts`, `src/agents/pi-tools.multi-root-guard.ts`, `src/agents/apply-patch.ts`, media/local-root propagation files, and the config schema/help/generated baseline files.
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: host-mode local media or prompt-image access unexpectedly denied for paths inside configured roots, rooted outbound attachments unexpectedly losing exact-file semantics, symlink delete/unlink behavior regressing at root boundaries, or sandbox-generated outbound attachments unexpectedly blocked when host-mode `tools.fs.roots` is configured.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: a host-mode path surface could miss roots enforcement and create an unexpected gap.
  - Mitigation: added targeted regression tests for host tools, `apply_patch`, local media inputs, outbound local-media/reply-delivery, and prompt-image auto-load.
- Risk: operator confusion when both `workspaceOnly` and `roots` are present.
  - Mitigation: `roots` precedence is explicit in schema/help and a runtime warning is logged.
- Risk: `exec` still bypasses FS roots through shell commands.
  - Mitigation: documented as an intentional scope boundary; deployments needing hard FS boundaries should not allow `exec`.

## AI assistance

- [x] AI-assisted
- Testing: locally revalidated on the current merged head (`7b771d448d`) with `pnpm check`, focused media/WhatsApp review-thread regressions, and the two prior PR-specific filtered tests; full `pnpm test` and `pnpm build` were not rerun on this final merged head; GitHub CI is green on `7b771d448d` (74 successful, 9 skipped) after the latest review fix.




## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `tools.fs.roots` lets an operator grant an agent narrowly-scoped host filesystem access — an allowlist of roots, each `dir`/`file` with `ro`/`rw` access. Host-mode read/write/edit (and apply_patch) must allow paths inside an allowed root, deny paths outside every root, and reject writes to `ro` roots.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at PR head `b4056fc332` (Node 22, macOS), driving the production host-mode filesystem tools against a real on-disk temporary directory tree (real `fs` syscalls; no fixtures or stubs).
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Created a real temp tree (an `rw` root `shared-rw`, an `ro` root `shared-ro`, and a `secret/` directory outside every root with a real file in it), built the production tool set via `createOpenClawCodingTools({ config: { tools: { fs: { roots: [...] } } } })`, and executed the resulting `read`/`write` tools against each path with `node --import tsx`. Live console output below.
- **Evidence after fix:**

```text
config tools.fs.roots:
  - { path: /tmp/fs-roots-proof-qTDt3u/shared-rw, kind: dir, access: rw }
  - { path: /tmp/fs-roots-proof-qTDt3u/shared-ro, kind: dir, access: ro }
workspace (not a root): /tmp/fs-roots-proof-qTDt3u/workspace
forbidden dir (not a root): /tmp/fs-roots-proof-qTDt3u/secret

=== host-mode reads ===
READ  inside rw root  (shared-rw/notes.txt)
      -> ALLOWED, content: "rw-root content"
READ  inside ro root  (shared-ro/policy.txt)
      -> ALLOWED, content: "ro-root content"
READ  OUTSIDE roots   (secret/passwords.txt)
      -> DENIED: Access denied: path '/tmp/fs-roots-proof-qTDt3u/secret/passwords.txt' is outside allowed filesystem roots

=== host-mode writes ===
WRITE inside rw root  (shared-rw/created.txt)
      -> ALLOWED, on disk: true
WRITE inside ro root  (shared-ro/hack.txt)
      -> DENIED: Access denied: path '/tmp/fs-roots-proof-qTDt3u/shared-ro/hack.txt' is inside read-only root '/tmp/fs-roots-proof-qTDt3u/shared-ro'  (on disk: false)
WRITE OUTSIDE roots   (secret/exfil.txt)
      -> DENIED: Access denied: path '/tmp/fs-roots-proof-qTDt3u/secret/exfil.txt' is outside allowed filesystem roots  (on disk: false)

verify: secret/passwords.txt still says: "TOP SECRET — outside every allowed root"
```

- **Observed result after fix:** Reads inside the `rw` and `ro` roots returned the real file contents. A read of `secret/passwords.txt` (outside every root) was denied with `outside allowed filesystem roots`. A write inside the `rw` root landed on disk (`on disk: true`); a write to the `ro` root was denied (`inside read-only root`) and a write outside every root was denied — both left nothing on disk (`on disk: false`), and the out-of-root secret file was byte-for-byte unchanged afterward.
- **What was not tested:** Sandbox mode is out of scope by design — sandbox ignores `tools.fs.roots` and relies on Docker isolation — so it was not exercised here. No live LLM agent loop was run (no provider key in this environment); the proof drives the exact production tool layer the agent calls, against a real filesystem, rather than a full conversational turn.

target.title: docs(gateway): document high-memory local Ollama compaction tuning
target.body:
﻿Fixes #55888

## What Problem This Solves

High-memory local Ollama users have existing compaction knobs that can keep more recent transcript in large-context sessions, but the local models guide did not explain the supported profile, threshold math, or safe verification path. Users had to infer why a 32k context model started memory flush or preflight compaction earlier than expected.

## Why This Change Was Made

This adds a focused docs-only profile to the local models guide for 32 GB / 64 GB+ Ollama setups. It documents the existing `agents.defaults.compaction` keys, explains the `contextWindow - reserveTokensFloor - memoryFlush.softThresholdTokens` threshold calculation, and keeps runtime defaults unchanged for lower-memory machines.

## User Impact

Users running larger local Ollama contexts can now tune compaction intentionally, see the expected 32,768 - 2,000 - 4,000 = 26,768 threshold example, and confirm the effective `contextWindow` and `threshold` in verbose Gateway logs before relying on a long session.

## Evidence

AI-assisted: yes. I used Codex to inspect the issue, source-backed threshold behavior, docs rules, and validation output. I reviewed the final diff and understand the change.

Validation run locally:

```text
git diff --check
# passed

node scripts/docs-list.js
# passed

node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs README.md
# Docs MDX check passed (684 files, 9324ms).

node scripts/docs-link-audit.mjs
# checked_internal_links=5464, broken_links=0

node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check
# failed on pre-existing docs/reference/templates/CLAUDE.md, not touched by this PR
```

Attempted the requested pnpm docs commands too, but local pnpm ran a dependency status/install path before the scripts and failed on Windows `EPERM` renames inside `node_modules` (`@types/node` / `@lit/context`). The equivalent Node docs scripts above were run directly and passed for the changed docs surface.
