target.title: fix(ui): improve accessible session history picker
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #95601.

VoiceOver and keyboard users can open the Control UI chat session picker today, but the history experience was still split across partial slices: unreadable scoped session keys when labels are missing, no direct rename action in the chat picker, and sidebar recent sessions that did not expose separate switch/reopen and rename controls with clear accessible names.

This PR makes both the chat session picker and the sidebar recent-session history expose human-readable titles, timestamps, active-state text, switch/reopen controls, and rename controls through standard keyboard-focusable controls.

## Summary

- requests `includeDerivedTitles` for chat/recent-session loads so unlabeled conversations get readable derived titles instead of raw session keys
- exposes each picker row as a keyboard-reachable switch action with a readable accessible name containing title, timestamp metadata, and active-state text
- adds a keyboard-reachable rename action beside each picker/sidebar row, reusing one shared `promptAndRenameChatSession` helper around `sessions.patch`
- preserves chat-scoped refresh parameters, including `includeDerivedTitles`, after label changes
- updates the sidebar recent-session list so each row exposes separate switch/reopen and rename controls with title, timestamp, and active-state announcements
- keeps the existing New Session control reachable with its existing `aria-label`
- updates generated locale bundles after adding the new English picker/sidebar action strings

## Accessibility Notes

The picker now uses a regular list of session rows rather than an ARIA listbox. Each row contains separate, focusable controls for:

- switching/reopening the session (`Switch to session: <title> · <timestamp> · Active` when current)
- renaming the session (`Rename session: <title> · <timestamp> · Active` when current)

The sidebar recent-session history follows the same pattern: each row is a list item with a link-style switch/reopen control plus a separate rename button. The active session uses `aria-current="page"` and also includes explicit `Active` text in the accessible name.

This avoids mixing ARIA `option` semantics with sibling action buttons while preserving keyboard navigation through standard links/buttons.

## Session Label Write Path

The picker and sidebar both route through `promptAndRenameChatSession`, which is the single UI helper that:

- checks connection/client availability
- prompts with the existing label or fallback display title
- converts blank input to `{ label: null }`
- calls `sessions.patch` with chat-scoped refresh options and `includeDerivedTitles`

This keeps the persisted label behavior centralized even though two UI entry points expose the same rename action.

## Evidence

- Added jsdom coverage that verifies derived titles render in the chat picker and the switch control accessible name includes title, timestamp metadata, and active-state text.
- Added jsdom coverage that verifies each picker row exposes a rename control and persists the new label through the shared rename helper while retaining chat-scoped refresh parameters.
- Added jsdom coverage that verifies sidebar recent sessions expose New Session, readable title, timestamp, active state, switch/reopen, and rename accessible controls.
- Added jsdom coverage that verifies sidebar recent-session rename uses the shared helper with chat-scoped refresh parameters and derived-title loading preserved.
- Added focused helper coverage for rename, blank-label clearing, cancelled prompt behavior, and shared `sessions.patch` arguments.
- Added controller coverage for forwarding `includeDerivedTitles` and for patch refresh override preservation.
- Ran `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat/session-rename.test.ts ui/src/ui/navigation.browser.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-render.assistant-avatar.test.ts`:
  - `ui/src/ui/navigation.browser.test.ts`: 14 passed
  - `ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts`: 81 passed
  - `ui/src/ui/app-render.assistant-avatar.test.ts`: 22 passed
  - `ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts`: 121 passed
  - `ui/src/ui/chat/session-rename.test.ts`: 3 passed
- Ran targeted `corepack pnpm format:check` on changed UI files: passed
- Ran targeted `OPENCLAW_OXLINT_SHARDS_SERIAL=1 node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json ...` on changed UI files: passed
- Ran `git diff --check`: passed

## Real Behavior Proof

- Exact-head browser proof run: <https://github.com/snowzlmbot/openclaw/actions/runs/28078695304>
- Artifact: `control-ui-session-history-proof-a7faf9d8ccab43c68d5af689d9b2fc62e6bafe2d`
- The proof checks out exact PR head `a7faf9d8ccab43c68d5af689d9b2fc62e6bafe2d`, starts the Control UI mock dev server, captures a screenshot (`control-ui-session-history.png`), and uploads DOM assertions (`control-ui-session-history-proof.json`).
- Verified DOM summary from the artifact:
  - New Session: `aria-label="New session"`
  - Sidebar switch/reopen: `Switch to session: Alpha planning · 33d ago · Active`
  - Sidebar rename: `Rename session: Alpha planning · 33d ago · Active`
  - Picker switch/reopen: `Switch to session: Alpha planning · openai/gpt-5.5 · 5/22/2026, 8:59:59 AM · Active`
  - Picker rename: `Rename session: Alpha planning · openai/gpt-5.5 · 5/22/2026, 8:59:59 AM · Active`
- All proof assertions passed for title readability, active-state announcement, switch/reopen controls, and rename controls in both picker and sidebar.

## Not Tested

- I did not run a live macOS VoiceOver pass locally. The PR adds source-level/jsdom coverage and requests exact-head visual proof for the visible Control UI behavior so maintainers can verify the branch without relying only on jsdom assertions.

target.title: fix(plugins): honor prerelease precedence in min-host-version checks
target.body:
## Summary

`parseMinHostVersionRequirement` (`src/plugins/min-host-version.ts`) accepts and preserves a **prerelease** floor — `>=2026.5.1-beta.1` parses to `minimumLabel: "2026.5.1-beta.1"` (a tested contract). But `checkMinHostVersion` compared versions with `parseSemver`/`isAtLeast` from `runtime-guard.ts`, whose regex `(\d+)\.(\d+)\.(\d+)` captures only the `major.minor.patch` triple and **drops the prerelease**. So the prerelease floor is parsed and stored but never honored:

- host `2026.5.1-beta.1` was treated as satisfying `>=2026.5.1-beta.3` (an older prerelease passes a newer-prerelease floor), and
- host `2026.5.1-beta.1` was treated as satisfying `>=2026.5.1` (a prerelease that precedes the stable release passes the stable floor).

Both should be **incompatible**. `checkMinHostVersion` gates plugin **load** (`manifest-registry.ts`), **install** (`install.ts`), and **update** (`update.ts`), so a host-incompatible plugin was loaded/installed/updated anyway.

### Changes
- `min-host-version.ts` — compare with the prerelease-aware sibling `parseComparableSemver`/`compareComparableSemver` (`src/infra/semver-compare.ts`); incompatible when `compareComparableSemver(current, minimum) < 0`. (`parseSemver`/`isAtLeast` stay in `runtime-guard.ts` for the Node-runtime checks that intentionally ignore prereleases.)
- `min-host-version.test.ts` — prerelease precedence cases.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: a prerelease host satisfied a newer-prerelease or stable floor it should fail. After the fix prerelease precedence is honored; stable-vs-stable, unknown-host, build-metadata, and leading-`v` behavior are unchanged.

Real environment tested: Node 22.22.1, macOS, no network/model. Vitest exercises the real exported `checkMinHostVersion`. BEFORE is `origin/main`'s file (swapped via `git show`).

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/min-host-version.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix:
```
checkMinHostVersion({ currentVersion: "2026.5.1-beta.1", minHostVersion: ">=2026.5.1-beta.3" })
  BEFORE: { ok: true, ... }              AFTER: { ok: false, kind: "incompatible", ... }
checkMinHostVersion({ currentVersion: "2026.5.1-beta.1", minHostVersion: ">=2026.5.1" })
  BEFORE: { ok: true, ... }              AFTER: { ok: false, kind: "incompatible", ... }
checkMinHostVersion({ currentVersion: "2026.5.1-beta.3", minHostVersion: ">=2026.5.1-beta.1" })
  BOTH: { ok: true, ... }                (newer prerelease still compatible)
```

Observed result after fix: prerelease floors are enforced; the new tests fail on `origin/main` and pass after, and all existing `min-host-version.test.ts` cases (equal/newer/older stable, unknown host, build metadata, leading `v`) stay green.

What was not tested: end-to-end plugin load/install gating (the test drives the real exported `checkMinHostVersion` those paths call).

## Additional checks
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/min-host-version.test.ts` passes; fail-before verified by swapping in `origin/main`'s file. `oxlint --tsconfig` and `tsgo:core` clean.

target.title: fix(video-generation): bound DashScope JSON response reads
target.body:
## Summary

Replace raw `response.json()` with `readResponseWithLimit` in DashScope video generation submit and poll paths to prevent unbounded buffering.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

**Behavior addressed**: Two unbounded `response.json()` calls replaced with `readResponseWithLimit` (16 MB cap).

**Real environment tested**: Linux, Node 24, OpenClaw main @ 0671c089

**Exact steps or command run after fix**:

```bash
node --import tsx proof-dashscope-video.mts
```

**Evidence after fix**:

```
cap: 16777216 bytes (16 MiB)
chunks pulled: 17
error: DashScope video-generation response exceeds 16777216 bytes
PASS: reader stopped at cap
```

The harness creates a streaming JSON body. The reader stops at 17 chunks (16 MB cap), cancels the stream, and throws. Before the fix, unbounded `response.json()` would have buffered the entire body.

**Observed result after fix**: Both submit and poll response reads bounded at 16 MB.

**What was not tested**: Live DashScope API endpoint was not tested.

## Risk

Low. `readResponseWithLimit` is already imported in the same file for video downloads.

target.title: fix(minimax-vlm): bound VLM API response reads
target.body:
## Summary

Replace raw `res.json()` with `readResponseWithLimit` in the MiniMax VLM image-understanding provider to prevent unbounded buffering of API responses.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

**Behavior addressed**: Unbounded `res.json()` replaced with `readResponseWithLimit` (16 MB cap).

**Real environment tested**: Linux, Node 24, OpenClaw main @ 0671c089

**Exact steps or command run after fix**:

```bash
node --import tsx proof-minimax-vlm.mts
```

**Evidence after fix**:

```
cap: 16777216 bytes (16 MiB)
chunks pulled: 17
error: MiniMax VLM response exceeds 16777216 bytes
PASS: reader stopped at cap, stream cancelled before full buffering
```

The harness creates a streaming 64 MB JSON body with 1 MB chunks. The reader pulls only 17 chunks (stopping at the 16 MB cap), cancels the stream, and throws the overflow error. Before the fix, `res.json()` would have buffered the entire 64 MB before parsing.

**Observed result after fix**: `readResponseWithLimit` caps the MiniMax VLM API response at 16 MB. Streaming bodies are cancelled at the cap instead of being fully buffered.

**What was not tested**: A live MiniMax VLM API call against api.minimax.io was not tested.

## Risk

Low. 16 MB cap matches the convention in `provider-http-errors.ts`. Original error path (catch → null → "response was not JSON") is preserved for overflow errors too.

target.title: fix(sessions): prune stale gateway model-run sessions
target.body:
## Summary

Adds `session.maintenance.modelRunPruneAfter` to clean up stale gateway model-run probe sessions (`agent:*:explicit:model-run-<uuid>`) that otherwise accumulate until they hit the global `maxEntries` cap and evict real sessions (#88632).

The default is **pressure-gated** rather than eager, so upgrades do not silently delete old probe-shaped rows from otherwise-healthy stores:

- `modelRunPruneAfter` **unset** → 24h retention, but model-run pruning only runs **when session-entry cap pressure is reached** (before the global stale-prune and `maxEntries` cap). This targets #88632's actual failure mode — probes contributing to cap pressure and displacing real sessions — without touching healthy stores.
- explicit duration/number (e.g. `"24h"`, `"48h"`) → opt into **unconditional** age-based cleanup whenever maintenance runs.
- `false` → disabled.

Pruning is gated consistently across the three maintenance paths (runtime write, load-time enforcement, `sessions cleanup`). For forced maintenance (`sessions cleanup` / explicit override) the cap applies immediately, so model-run pruning is aligned to the same cap-eviction threshold there — stale probes are removed before the forced cap can evict a real session in their place.

Hardening:

- `isGatewayModelRunSessionKey` matches only strict canonical `agent:<id>:explicit:model-run-<uuid>` keys, rejecting unscoped / empty-agent / extra-segment / whitespace keys; normal direct, group, thread, cron, hook, heartbeat, ACP, and sub-agent sessions are never eligible.
- An invalid explicit `modelRunPruneAfter` value fails closed (no pruning) instead of silently enabling the 24h default; the strict session schema rejects invalid explicit values.

`modelRunPruned` / `prune-model-run` are reported separately in cleanup summaries and dry-run plans.

Fixes #88632

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: stale gateway `model-run-<uuid>` probe sessions are pruned before the `maxEntries` cap can evict real sessions, but only under cap pressure when `modelRunPruneAfter` is unset, while explicit config prunes unconditionally and healthy stores are left untouched.
- Real environment tested: local macOS checkout at head `0002eb0770e80c257ae57905ea60487c24a9cd6a`, Node 26.0.0, built CLI via `node scripts/build-all.mjs cliStartup`, isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME` / `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` / `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH` and a temp session store under `/tmp/openclaw-91057-proof`.
- Store fixture (re-created before each run): one stale model-run probe (`...174001`, updated 2 days ago), one recent model-run probe (`...174002`, updated now), one durable control (`agent:main:explicit:durable-control`).
- Exact command per scenario:

```console
$ OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/openclaw-91057-proof/home \
  OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-91057-proof/state \
  OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=<scenario config> \
  node openclaw.mjs sessions cleanup --enforce --json \
  --store /tmp/openclaw-91057-proof/sessions.json
```

- Evidence after fix (copied from the real built CLI runs):

**A — unset default, `maxEntries: 500` (no cap pressure):**

```json
{ "beforeCount": 3, "afterCount": 3, "modelRunPruned": 0, "pruned": 0, "capped": 0 }
```

remaining keys: stale probe, recent probe, durable control all retained — the pressure-gated default does not delete probe-shaped rows from a healthy store.

**B — unset default, `maxEntries: 2` (cap pressure):**

```json
{ "beforeCount": 3, "afterCount": 2, "modelRunPruned": 1, "pruned": 0, "capped": 0 }
```

remaining keys: recent probe + durable control. The stale probe was removed via model-run pruning (`modelRunPruned: 1`) **before** the cap (`capped: 0`), so the durable control survived instead of being evicted.

**C — explicit `modelRunPruneAfter: "24h"`, `maxEntries: 500` (no pressure):**

```json
{ "beforeCount": 3, "afterCount": 2, "modelRunPruned": 1, "pruned": 0, "capped": 0 }
```

remaining keys: recent probe + durable control. Explicit config prunes the stale probe unconditionally even with no cap pressure.

- Observed result: default behavior is pressure-gated (A vs B), explicit config is unconditional (C), the strict probe predicate left the durable control and the recent probe untouched in every case, and under pressure the stale probe is removed by model-run pruning rather than by the cap evicting a real session.
- What was not tested: no live remote gateway model inference call was required; the behavior was exercised through the real built OpenClaw CLI against an isolated session store. No channel, credential, model provider, or gateway transport behavior is changed.

## Testing

- `node scripts/build-all.mjs cliStartup`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.runtime-config.config.ts src/config/sessions/store.pruning.test.ts src/config/sessions/store.pruning.integration.test.ts src/config/zod-schema.session-maintenance-extensions.test.ts src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts src/config/schema.base.generated.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.commands.config.ts src/commands/sessions-cleanup.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugin-sdk/session-store-runtime.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:core:test`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check` on touched files

## Current status (2026-06-24)

Default model-run pruning is now **pressure-gated when unset** (Option C) instead of unconditionally on after upgrade: it removes strict `model-run-<uuid>` probe rows only when they would contribute to global cap pressure, so existing healthy stores are not eagerly mutated on upgrade. Explicit `modelRunPruneAfter` opts into unconditional age cleanup; `false` disables.

This directly addresses the earlier `compatibility` / `session-state` merge-risk concerns: the only default-on behavior is removing strict ephemeral probe rows at the same point the cap would otherwise evict real sessions. A self-review pass also fixed a forced-cleanup edge where `sessions cleanup` capped immediately while model-run pruning was still high-water gated, which could let stale probes survive while real sessions were capped — model-run pruning is now aligned to the immediate-cap threshold on forced paths, with a regression test. `maintainerCanModify` is enabled, so this branch is takeover-ready.

commands.author: wangmiao0668000666

target.title: fix(chutes-oauth): bound Chutes OAuth JSON response reads
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`src/agents/chutes-oauth.ts` parses the Chutes OAuth token-exchange, token-refresh,
and userinfo responses with raw `await response.json()` calls (3 sites: lines 118,
158, 229). None of them cap the byte read, so a misbehaving Chutes endpoint or
proxy that streams an unbounded body can pull unlimited bytes into the agent
process and trigger OOM before the JSON.parse runs.

The shared `readProviderJsonResponse` helper in
`openclaw/plugin-sdk/provider-http` (used by the bound-stream family merged
in #95926, #96036, #96136, #96144) caps the read at 16 MiB and surfaces an
actionable error before OOM. This PR routes the three Chutes OAuth response
sites through that helper, closing the chutes-oauth half of the
unbounded-response.json() family that #96144 called out as "Out of scope".

## Why This Change Was Made

- Cap is symmetric with the existing `cancelUnreadResponseBody` pattern already
  in `fetchChutesUserInfo` (line 100-104, line 115). Bounded read is the
  natural follow-up.
- Bounded-read helper is generic, owned by `provider-http`, and already
  proven on six upstream Alix-007 bound-stream PRs plus the recent
  image-generation / video-generation / dashscope sites.
- Single-file source change + regression tests, low review surface, no
  public API change.
- The three error labels (`"Chutes token exchange"`, `"Chutes token refresh"`,
  `"Chutes userinfo"`) are reusable for both `readProviderJsonResponse` and
  `assertOkOrThrowHttpError` if downstream sites adopt the same pattern.

## Changes

- `src/agents/chutes-oauth.ts` — `+6/-5` — import
  `readProviderJsonResponse` from `./provider-http-errors.js`; replace
  three `(await response.json()) as Type` calls with
  `await readProviderJsonResponse<Type>(response, "<Chutes label>")`.
- `src/agents/chutes-oauth.flow.test.ts` — `+111/-0` — add a new
  `describe("chutes-oauth bounded reader")` block with three regression
  tests: 64 MiB token exchange rejected, 64 MiB token refresh rejected,
  64 MiB userinfo rejected, all with the canonical
  `<label>: JSON response exceeds 16777216 bytes` overflow error.
- `scripts/repro/issue-chutes-oauth-bounded-read.mjs` — new — standalone
  repro script that drives the production `readProviderJsonResponse`
  directly (no vitest mock) against a 78-byte valid envelope, three
  64 MiB hostile bodies (one per production label), and a raw
  `response.json()` negative control.

## Evidence

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/chutes-oauth.test.ts src/agents/chutes-oauth.flow.test.ts`
  — 15/15 passed (6 + 6 existing + 3 new bounded-reader tests)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/chutes-oauth.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.chutes.test.ts`
  — 2/2 passed (downstream consumers of `CHUTES_TOKEN_ENDPOINT` constant
  unaffected)
- `npx oxlint --import-plugin --config .oxlintrc.json` on the 2 changed
  TS files — exit 0
- `pnpm exec tsx scripts/repro/issue-chutes-oauth-bounded-read.mjs` —
  exit 0 (5 assertions: valid + 3 hostile + negative control)

Sample failure when the body exceeds the 16 MiB cap (matches the helper
behavior verified in #96144):
```
Error: Chutes token exchange: JSON response exceeds 16777216 bytes
```

## Real behavior proof

The repro script drives the production `readProviderJsonResponse`
(defined at `src/agents/provider-http-errors.ts:297`) directly with no
vitest mock. The three hostile-body assertions each use a different
production label (`"Chutes token exchange"`, `"Chutes token refresh"`,
`"Chutes userinfo"`) so the bounded reader is exercised against the
exact label values the production sites pass.

```
$ pnpm exec tsx scripts/repro/issue-chutes-oauth-bounded-read.mjs
=== Reproduction for chutes-oauth bounded JSON response cap policy ===
PROVIDER_JSON_RESPONSE_MAX_BYTES = 16777216 bytes
PASS  valid Chutes token exchange response: accepted (78 bytes, 3 fields parsed)
PASS  hostile 64 MiB token exchange response: rejected with "Chutes token exchange hostile: JSON response exceeds 16777216 bytes"
PASS  hostile 64 MiB token refresh response: rejected with "Chutes token refresh hostile: JSON response exceeds 16777216 bytes"
PASS  hostile 64 MiB userinfo response: rejected with "Chutes userinfo hostile: JSON response exceeds 16777216 bytes"
PASS  negative control: raw response.json() on 64 MiB body failed with "SyntaxError" (no bounded-reader wrapping)
=== All repro assertions passed ===
```

The three hostile assertions are independent: each streams a fresh 64 MiB
body and asserts the exact label-prefixed overflow error. The negative
control proves the bounded read is the source of the cap (raw
`response.json()` on the same body buffers the full payload and only
fails on JSON parse, not on size).

## Out of scope (separate follow-ups)

- `extensions/*/src/**` files with the same `response.json()` pattern
  (Alix-007 family of unbounded reads). Some are already capped
  (msteams/src/graph.ts, google-meet) and the rest are queued for
  per-extension follow-up PRs.
- The `assertOkOrThrowHttpError` calls in the same file (lines 153-156
  and 224-227) are kept verbatim — they cover the HTTP-error path, not
  the oversize-body path, and the bounded reader runs after the HTTP
  status check.

target.title: fix #96116: memory index: openclaw memory index --force exits early without processing full backlog, no error logged
target.body:
## Summary

- Align `openclaw memory status` session totals with the same usage-counted transcript filename predicate used by session indexing.
- Add a regression test covering primary transcripts, reset archives, checkpoints, and trajectory artifacts.

## What Problem This Solves

- `openclaw memory status` could show a persistent session backlog after indexing because it counted every `.jsonl` file under the sessions directory.
- Session indexing only processes usage-counted transcript filenames, so checkpoint and trajectory runtime artifacts were included in the status denominator even though `openclaw memory index --force` is not supposed to index them.
- Why it matters / User impact: users could see `sessions · indexed/total` remain below total after a successful index run and conclude the indexer exited early or silently skipped files.

## User Impact

- Users with large session directories get a status total that reflects the transcript set the indexer can actually process.
- `memory status` no longer reports non-indexable checkpoint or trajectory artifacts as unindexed backlog.
- The patch does not change what gets indexed; it only fixes the reporting denominator.

## Origin / follow-up

- Refs #96116.
- The issue report described `memory index --force` apparently stopping early while `memory status` kept showing a sessions backlog.
- This PR addresses the visible status-denominator/reporting mismatch that can make a completed index run still look incomplete.
- This PR does not claim to fully prove the reporter's larger Gemini `memory index --force` early-exit/no-error path. If maintainers think that path can still occur after this denominator fix, #96116 should stay open or be split into a follow-up for live/provider-specific reproduction.

## Competition / linked PR analysis

- Linked PR(s) for #96116: none found in the issue-linked open PR scan.
- Related denominator PR: #95452 also changes the same `scanSessionFiles()` predicate line for an older denominator issue.
- Canonical landing choice: maintainers should choose one denominator fix as the canonical landing path. This PR is a focused #96116 status-denominator patch with a regression test for primary/reset transcripts versus checkpoint and trajectory artifacts.
- If #95452 lands first: this PR's production change may be superseded, but the regression scenario here should be preserved or migrated so the status denominator remains aligned with the indexable session transcript corpus.
- If this PR lands first: it should be treated as a denominator/reporting fix, not as proof that every possible large Gemini `--force` early-exit/no-error path has been eliminated.
- Why this patch is canonical for its scoped symptom: it reuses the existing `isUsageCountedSessionTranscriptFileName` source-of-truth instead of adding another status-only filename rule.
- Local real behavior proof advantage: the proof runs the real memory status CLI boundary against a temporary OpenClaw state directory with both indexable transcripts and non-indexable session artifacts.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `openclaw memory status` session totals now count only the files that session indexing treats as usage-counted transcripts.
- **Real environment tested:** Linux, Node v22.22.0, pnpm v11.2.2, repository worktree `/media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-96175`, temporary OpenClaw state directory, `memorySearch.provider: "none"` FTS-only mode to keep the proof inside the local status boundary.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```sh
pnpm --dir "/media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-96175" exec tsx "/media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-96175-evidence/status-session-total-proof.mts"
pnpm --dir "/media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-96175" exec vitest extensions/memory-core/src/cli.test.ts --run
pnpm --dir "/media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-96175" exec oxfmt --check extensions/memory-core/src/cli.runtime.ts extensions/memory-core/src/cli.test.ts
```

- **Evidence after fix:**

```text
Memory index complete.
Memory Search (main)
Provider: none (requested: none)
Model: none
Sources: sessions
Indexed: 2/2 files · 2 chunks
By source:
  sessions · 2/2 files · 2 chunks
proof worktree=/media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-96175
proof indexedLine=Indexed: 2/2 files · 2 chunks
proof sessionsLine=  sessions · 2/2 files · 2 chunks
PASS: memory status totals match the usage-counted session transcript set used by indexing.
```

```text
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  74 passed (74)
```

```text
Checking formatting...
All matched files use the correct format.
```

- **Observed result after fix:** with two indexable transcript files (`active.jsonl`, `archived.jsonl.reset...`) and two non-indexable `.jsonl` artifacts (checkpoint and trajectory), status reports `Indexed: 2/2 files` and `sessions · 2/2 files`, matching the indexing file set.
- **What was not tested:** embedding quality, recall ranking, watcher scheduling, storage migration paths, third-party provider/API behavior, and the reporter's large Gemini `memory index --force` early-exit/no-error live path were not tested because this fix is confined to local session filename classification for status reporting.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix for the CLI status denominator/reporting mismatch.

## Review findings addressed

- RF-001: disclosed the canonical choice between this PR and #95452. No code repair is required on this branch unless maintainers choose to combine or migrate the regression coverage.
- RF-002: changed the issue reference from closing syntax to scoped reference language. This PR now states that it addresses the status-denominator/reporting symptom and does not claim complete live proof for the larger Gemini `--force` early-exit/no-error path.
- RF-003: documented that #95452 touches the same production line and that maintainers should choose one denominator landing path while preserving this regression scenario.
- RF-004: documented the maintainer-decision boundary: linked issue closure scope and canonical PR choice are decision items, not narrow code defects in this branch.
- Maintainer-ready confidence: high for the narrow reporting bug because the patch reuses the existing indexing filename predicate and exercises the real status CLI boundary.
- Patch quality notes: one production predicate change plus one regression test; no indexing flow, embedding selection, storage schema, transcript archive naming, or watcher behavior changed.

## Regression Test Plan

- Target test file: `extensions/memory-core/src/cli.test.ts`
- Scenario locked in: status totals include primary and reset session transcripts but exclude checkpoint and trajectory `.jsonl` artifacts.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the regression targets `runMemoryCli(["status"])`, the display path that produced the misleading denominator, without mocking a different filename classifier.

```text
Before the patch, the new regression failed with:
Indexed: 2/3 files · 2 chunks
sessions · 2/3 files · 2 chunks

After the patch:
Tests  74 passed (74)
```

## Merge risk

- Merge-risk assessment: medium unless maintainers explicitly choose the canonical denominator PR and issue closure scope.
- Public contract / schema compatibility: no public config schema, transcript file format, storage schema, command option, or indexing corpus rule changes. The `.jsonl` mention here is only the local transcript filename extension involved in the existing mismatch.
- Failure mode if wrong: status could under-count or over-count local session backlog; it would not delete, rewrite, or newly index any session files.
- Merge-scope note: merging this PR should not silently close broader provider/live-reproduction work unless maintainers decide the denominator mismatch fully explains #96116.

## Risk / Compatibility

- Risk labels considered: low-risk CLI reporting change; no data migration; no embedding selection behavior change.
- Risk explanation: users may see lower session totals when checkpoint or trajectory artifacts are present, but those files were never part of the session indexing corpus.
- Why acceptable: the new total matches the source-of-truth predicate already used by `listSessionFilesForAgent`, so status reflects actual indexable backlog instead of raw `.jsonl` file count.

## What was not changed

- What did NOT change: this patch does not change memory indexing, forced reindex selection, batch indexing, vector/FTS storage, session watcher behavior, transcript archive naming, reset/deleted archive inclusion, or checkpoint/trajectory exclusion rules.
- Architecture / source-of-truth check: the source-of-truth for session files is `listSessionFilesForAgent`, which filters with `isUsageCountedSessionTranscriptFileName`. That predicate already defines the usage-counted corpus by including primary/reset/deleted transcripts and excluding checkpoint, trajectory, backup, and metadata files. Status now uses that same predicate for its session total scan.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** the source-of-truth for indexable session transcripts is `listSessionFilesForAgent` plus `isUsageCountedSessionTranscriptFileName`, but `scanSessionFiles()` in the memory CLI status path owned a separate filename rule and counted files with `entry.name.endsWith(".jsonl")`. Because the status rule accepted checkpoint and trajectory runtime artifacts that the indexing rule rejects, status counted those artifacts as backlog even though a forced index was never supposed to process them, so the displayed total could remain unreachable after a successful index.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** the status denominator is produced inside `scanSessionFiles()`, and the invariant it must preserve is "status total equals the indexable session transcript corpus." Replacing its status-only `.jsonl` check with the shared usage-counted transcript predicate fixes that invariant at the source. The patch does not add ad hoc exclusions or change indexer semantics; it makes status report the same corpus the indexer already uses.

target.title: fix(ui): improve accessible session history picker
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #95601.

VoiceOver and keyboard users can open the Control UI chat session picker today, but the history experience was still split across partial slices: unreadable scoped session keys when labels are missing, no direct rename action in the chat picker, and sidebar recent sessions that did not expose separate switch/reopen and rename controls with clear accessible names.

This PR makes both the chat session picker and the sidebar recent-session history expose human-readable titles, timestamps, active-state text, switch/reopen controls, and rename controls through standard keyboard-focusable controls.

## Summary

- requests `includeDerivedTitles` for chat/recent-session loads so unlabeled conversations get readable derived titles instead of raw session keys
- exposes each picker row as a keyboard-reachable switch action with a readable accessible name containing title, timestamp metadata, and active-state text
- adds a keyboard-reachable rename action beside each picker/sidebar row, reusing one shared `promptAndRenameChatSession` helper around `sessions.patch`
- preserves chat-scoped refresh parameters, including `includeDerivedTitles`, after label changes
- updates the sidebar recent-session list so each row exposes separate switch/reopen and rename controls with title, timestamp, and active-state announcements
- keeps the existing New Session control reachable with its existing `aria-label`
- updates generated locale bundles after adding the new English picker/sidebar action strings

## Accessibility Notes

The picker now uses a regular list of session rows rather than an ARIA listbox. Each row contains separate, focusable controls for:

- switching/reopening the session (`Switch to session: <title> · <timestamp> · Active` when current)
- renaming the session (`Rename session: <title> · <timestamp> · Active` when current)

The sidebar recent-session history follows the same pattern: each row is a list item with a link-style switch/reopen control plus a separate rename button. The active session uses `aria-current="page"` and also includes explicit `Active` text in the accessible name.

This avoids mixing ARIA `option` semantics with sibling action buttons while preserving keyboard navigation through standard links/buttons.

## Session Label Write Path

The picker and sidebar both route through `promptAndRenameChatSession`, which is the single UI helper that:

- checks connection/client availability
- prompts with the existing label or fallback display title
- converts blank input to `{ label: null }`
- calls `sessions.patch` with chat-scoped refresh options and `includeDerivedTitles`

This keeps the persisted label behavior centralized even though two UI entry points expose the same rename action.

## Evidence

- Added jsdom coverage that verifies derived titles render in the chat picker and the switch control accessible name includes title, timestamp metadata, and active-state text.
- Added jsdom coverage that verifies each picker row exposes a rename control and persists the new label through the shared rename helper while retaining chat-scoped refresh parameters.
- Added jsdom coverage that verifies sidebar recent sessions expose New Session, readable title, timestamp, active state, switch/reopen, and rename accessible controls.
- Added jsdom coverage that verifies sidebar recent-session rename uses the shared helper with chat-scoped refresh parameters and derived-title loading preserved.
- Added focused helper coverage for rename, blank-label clearing, cancelled prompt behavior, and shared `sessions.patch` arguments.
- Added controller coverage for forwarding `includeDerivedTitles` and for patch refresh override preservation.
- Ran `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat/session-rename.test.ts ui/src/ui/navigation.browser.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-render.assistant-avatar.test.ts`:
  - `ui/src/ui/navigation.browser.test.ts`: 14 passed
  - `ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts`: 81 passed
  - `ui/src/ui/app-render.assistant-avatar.test.ts`: 22 passed
  - `ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts`: 121 passed
  - `ui/src/ui/chat/session-rename.test.ts`: 3 passed
- Ran targeted `corepack pnpm format:check` on changed UI files: passed
- Ran targeted `OPENCLAW_OXLINT_SHARDS_SERIAL=1 node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json ...` on changed UI files: passed
- Ran `git diff --check`: passed

## Real Behavior Proof

- Exact-head browser proof run: <https://github.com/snowzlmbot/openclaw/actions/runs/28078695304>
- Artifact: `control-ui-session-history-proof-a7faf9d8ccab43c68d5af689d9b2fc62e6bafe2d`
- The proof checks out exact PR head `a7faf9d8ccab43c68d5af689d9b2fc62e6bafe2d`, starts the Control UI mock dev server, captures a screenshot (`control-ui-session-history.png`), and uploads DOM assertions (`control-ui-session-history-proof.json`).
- Verified DOM summary from the artifact:
  - New Session: `aria-label="New session"`
  - Sidebar switch/reopen: `Switch to session: Alpha planning · 33d ago · Active`
  - Sidebar rename: `Rename session: Alpha planning · 33d ago · Active`
  - Picker switch/reopen: `Switch to session: Alpha planning · openai/gpt-5.5 · 5/22/2026, 8:59:59 AM · Active`
  - Picker rename: `Rename session: Alpha planning · openai/gpt-5.5 · 5/22/2026, 8:59:59 AM · Active`
- All proof assertions passed for title readability, active-state announcement, switch/reopen controls, and rename controls in both picker and sidebar.

## Not Tested

- I did not run a live macOS VoiceOver pass locally. The PR adds source-level/jsdom coverage and requests exact-head visual proof for the visible Control UI behavior so maintainers can verify the branch without relying only on jsdom assertions.

commands.review_summary:
The PR rewrites agent-core prompt-template slash-command argument parsing and adds regression coverage for apostrophes, quoted spans, substitutions, and unmatched double quotes.

PR surface: Source +108, Tests +160. Total +268 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main treats every apostrophe as quote syntax before `$ARGUMENTS` and positional substitution, so prose like `don't it's broken now` is deterministically corrupted.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can add the missing compatibility test and adjust the single-quote closer rule without choosing product direction.

target.title: fix(agents): keep apostrophes in slash-command prompt-template arguments
target.body:
## Summary

- `parseCommandArgs` (`packages/agent-core/src/harness/prompt-template-arguments.ts`) expands `$ARGUMENTS` / `$@` / `$1..$N` in user prompt templates and custom slash commands. It does shell-style quote parsing with **no escape and no unbalanced-quote recovery**, so a lone `'` (an ordinary apostrophe in English prose: `user's`, `it's`, `don't`) opens a quote that runs to the next apostrophe (or end of string). The apostrophe is dropped and the following whitespace is swallowed into one token. The corrupted tokens then feed `$ARGUMENTS` (`args.join(" ")`) and the positional placeholders, so the model **silently receives a mangled instruction** with no error or warning.
  - `/cmd don't it's broken now` + a `[$1][$2]` template collapses to `ARG1=[dont its] ARG2=[broken]`: both apostrophes dropped, placeholders shifted.
- Reach: shipped and default-on. `AgentSession.prompt()` expands templates by default (`expandPromptTemplates ?? true`) and the **expanded text becomes the user message sent to the model**. Templates are real user `.md` files loaded by `loadPromptTemplates` from `<agentDir>/prompts`, `<cwd>/.openclaw/prompts`, and explicit prompt paths.
- Fix: single-quote handling is now an explicit shell-style grammar with no hardcoded contraction word list. A word-attached apostrophe is a delimiter only when it opens a concatenation that closes at a later trailing apostrophe (`foo'bar baz'`), and it yields to any nearer apostrophe that owns such a closer, so a leading prose word keeps its apostrophe (`y'all foo'bar baz'`, `don't 'quoted text' next`). A standalone or assignment-attached apostrophe prefers a later whitespace-spanning quote (`'echo one two'`, `foo='bar baz'`) and otherwise closes at the first apostrophe that is not an English contraction suffix, so shell-style concatenation still works before suffix text (`'foo'bar`, `'foo bar'baz` -> `foo barbaz`) while an unmatched leading quote before a contraction stays literal (`'review don't fail`). An apostrophe with no matching closer stays literal. Irregular contractions are recognized structurally rather than by an enumerated list, so `y'all`, `ma'am`, `o'clock`, `ne'er`, `cap'n`, and `y'know` are all preserved; only a small fixed set of English contraction suffixes (`d`, `ll`, `m`, `re`, `s`, `t`, `ve`) is consulted to tell a contraction apostrophe from a closing quote that precedes suffix text. Double-quote tokenization is unchanged from current main, including the unmatched case (`say "hello world` -> `["say", "hello world"]`). This replaces the prior revision's hardcoded `proseContractionWords` set while preserving all other shipped quote behavior.
- One canonical change in `parseCommandArgs`; both consumers (`expandPromptTemplate` in `src/agents/sessions/prompt-templates.ts` and `formatPromptTemplateInvocation` in `packages/agent-core`) benefit with no API change. The separate `src/auto-reply/commands-registry.ts` `parseCommandArgs` (native channel commands) is a different function and is left untouched.

## Tests

- `packages/agent-core/src/harness/prompt-templates.test.ts`: added cases covering apostrophes preserved in parsed tokens and through `$ARGUMENTS` / positional placeholders; multiple contractions; shell-style quoted spans embedded in a token (`--title="two words"`, `foo='bar baz'`); bare embedded single-quote concatenation (`foo'bar baz'`); a multi-word quoted span that closes before suffix text (`'foo bar'baz` -> `foo barbaz`, preserving current main); an unmatched double quote that groups to the end of input (`say "hello world`, `"unterminated`, `a"b`); a plural possessive next to a later quoted span (`fix users' 'quoted text'`); apostrophes after non-ASCII word characters (`José's don't fail`); an unmatched leading quote that must not close on a later contraction (`'review don't fail`); a contraction inside a balanced quoted phrase (`'it's a test'`); a contraction immediately before a later standalone single-quoted span (`don't 'quoted text' next`); irregular contractions before a later attached single-quote span (`y'all`, `ma'am`, `o'clock`); and a generality case proving any prose contraction is preserved without a fixed word list (`ne'er`, `cap'n`, `y'know`, `fo'c'sle`, plus closer-side `'y'all here'`). The existing quoted/multiline and placeholder-safety cases are unchanged and green (24/24).
- `src/agents/sessions/prompt-templates.test.ts` (`expandPromptTemplate`) unchanged and green.
- Touched suites run locally with the repo runner (`prompt-templates.test.ts` 24/24); tsgo core, type-aware oxlint, and oxfmt clean on the touched files.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: a user-authored custom slash command / prompt template invoked with ordinary prose containing an apostrophe silently lost the apostrophe (and, for positional `$N` templates, collapsed arguments and shifted later placeholders), so the model received a corrupted instruction. Covers prose contractions of every shape including irregular ones the parser does not enumerate (`y'all`, `o'clock`, `ne'er`, `cap'n`, `y'know`) and a contraction inside a balanced span (`'y'all here'`). Shipped quote behavior is preserved and verified to stay byte-identical to current main: shell-style concatenation including a multi-word span before suffix text (`'foo bar'baz` -> `foo barbaz`) and unmatched double quotes (`say "hello world`).
- Real environment tested: Linux, Node 22.19, exercising the actual production functions `loadPromptTemplates` + `expandPromptTemplate` (the same expansion path `AgentSession.prompt()` uses to build the outbound user message) over a real on-disk prompt directory (`<agentDir>/prompts/*.md`).
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: wrote `<tmp>/prompts/cmd.md` = `ARG1=[$1] ARG2=[$2] ARG3=[$3] ARG4=[$4]`; loaded it via `loadPromptTemplates({ cwd, agentDir, promptPaths, includeDefaults: false })`; called `expandPromptTemplate("/cmd <input>", templates)` for each input below; ran the script over the **current PR head** (`d7be396cb6`) and, for the before column, over the `origin/main` parser source (`substituteArgs` is identical, so only `parseCommandArgs` differs) with the rest of the path identical.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): copied live output from running the real `expandPromptTemplate` over the on-disk template on the current PR head `d7be396cb6`:

```
TEMPLATES LOADED: ["cmd"]
USER TYPES : /cmd don't it's broken now
MODEL GETS : ARG1=[don't] ARG2=[it's] ARG3=[broken] ARG4=[now]

USER TYPES : /cmd --title="two words" next
MODEL GETS : ARG1=[--title=two words] ARG2=[next] ARG3=[] ARG4=[]

USER TYPES : /cmd say "hello world
MODEL GETS : ARG1=[say] ARG2=[hello world] ARG3=[] ARG4=[]

USER TYPES : /cmd 'foo bar'baz next
MODEL GETS : ARG1=[foo barbaz] ARG2=[next] ARG3=[] ARG4=[]

USER TYPES : /cmd José's don't fail
MODEL GETS : ARG1=[José's] ARG2=[don't] ARG3=[fail] ARG4=[]

USER TYPES : /cmd 'review don't fail
MODEL GETS : ARG1=['review] ARG2=[don't] ARG3=[fail] ARG4=[]

USER TYPES : /cmd 'it's a test' next
MODEL GETS : ARG1=[it's a test] ARG2=[next] ARG3=[] ARG4=[]

USER TYPES : /cmd don't 'quoted text' next
MODEL GETS : ARG1=[don't] ARG2=[quoted text] ARG3=[next] ARG4=[]

USER TYPES : /cmd y'all foo'bar baz' next
MODEL GETS : ARG1=[y'all] ARG2=[foobar baz] ARG3=[next] ARG4=[]

USER TYPES : /cmd o'clock foo'bar baz' next
MODEL GETS : ARG1=[o'clock] ARG2=[foobar baz] ARG3=[next] ARG4=[]

USER TYPES : /cmd ne'er foo'bar baz' next
MODEL GETS : ARG1=[ne'er] ARG2=[foobar baz] ARG3=[next] ARG4=[]

USER TYPES : /cmd cap'n foo'bar baz' next
MODEL GETS : ARG1=[cap'n] ARG2=[foobar baz] ARG3=[next] ARG4=[]

USER TYPES : /cmd y'know foo'bar baz' next
MODEL GETS : ARG1=[y'know] ARG2=[foobar baz] ARG3=[next] ARG4=[]

USER TYPES : /cmd 'y'all here'
MODEL GETS : ARG1=[y'all here] ARG2=[] ARG3=[] ARG4=[]
```

- Observed result after fix: apostrophes survive end to end for prose contractions of every shape with no contraction word list in the parser: regular (`don't`, `it's`), irregular (`y'all`, `o'clock`, `ne'er`, `cap'n`, `y'know`), and a contraction inside a balanced span (`'y'all here'` groups to one argument). Shipped behavior is unchanged: a multi-word quoted span before suffix text still concatenates (`'foo bar'baz` -> `foo barbaz`), an unmatched leading quote before a contraction stays literal (`'review don't fail`), and an unmatched double quote groups to the end of input (`say "hello world`). The model receives the exact instruction the user typed.
- What was not tested: the separate native-channel `src/auto-reply/commands-registry.ts` `parseCommandArgs` (a different function, untouched); a fully wired gateway round-trip through a live provider (the proof drives the exact expansion function that produces the user message rather than issuing a model call).
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: none specific; the expansion is deterministic and reproduced on demand.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): the same script over the `origin/main` parser (unpatched) corrupts every prose case; the preserved forms (`'foo bar'baz`, `say "hello world`) are identical before and after, confirming compatibility:

```
TEMPLATES LOADED: ["cmd"]
USER TYPES : /cmd don't it's broken now
MODEL GETS : ARG1=[dont its] ARG2=[broken] ARG3=[now] ARG4=[]

USER TYPES : /cmd --title="two words" next
MODEL GETS : ARG1=[--title=two words] ARG2=[next] ARG3=[] ARG4=[]

USER TYPES : /cmd say "hello world
MODEL GETS : ARG1=[say] ARG2=[hello world] ARG3=[] ARG4=[]

USER TYPES : /cmd 'foo bar'baz next
MODEL GETS : ARG1=[foo barbaz] ARG2=[next] ARG3=[] ARG4=[]

USER TYPES : /cmd José's don't fail
MODEL GETS : ARG1=[Josés dont] ARG2=[fail] ARG3=[] ARG4=[]

USER TYPES : /cmd 'review don't fail
MODEL GETS : ARG1=[review dont] ARG2=[fail] ARG3=[] ARG4=[]

USER TYPES : /cmd 'it's a test' next
MODEL GETS : ARG1=[its] ARG2=[a] ARG3=[test next] ARG4=[]

USER TYPES : /cmd don't 'quoted text' next
MODEL GETS : ARG1=[dont quoted] ARG2=[text next] ARG3=[] ARG4=[]

USER TYPES : /cmd y'all foo'bar baz' next
MODEL GETS : ARG1=[yall foobar] ARG2=[baz next] ARG3=[] ARG4=[]

USER TYPES : /cmd o'clock foo'bar baz' next
MODEL GETS : ARG1=[oclock foobar] ARG2=[baz next] ARG3=[] ARG4=[]

USER TYPES : /cmd ne'er foo'bar baz' next
MODEL GETS : ARG1=[neer foobar] ARG2=[baz next] ARG3=[] ARG4=[]

USER TYPES : /cmd cap'n foo'bar baz' next
MODEL GETS : ARG1=[capn foobar] ARG2=[baz next] ARG3=[] ARG4=[]

USER TYPES : /cmd y'know foo'bar baz' next
MODEL GETS : ARG1=[yknow foobar] ARG2=[baz next] ARG3=[] ARG4=[]

USER TYPES : /cmd 'y'all here'
MODEL GETS : ARG1=[yall] ARG2=[here] ARG3=[] ARG4=[]
```

target.title: [AI-assisted] Add session history family lookup
target.body:
## Problem

When a threaded conversation has already been split across reset transcripts, `chat.history` and the `sessions_history` tool only read the currently active transcript. The session store can already retain the family through `usageFamilySessionIds`, but history readers do not use that chain and do not discover archived `*.jsonl.reset.<timestamp>` topic transcripts.

That means an agent/tool can be looking at the correct logical Telegram/Slack/Discord thread and still miss earlier turns after session rollover.

## Fix

Add an opt-in family history path:

- `chat.history` accepts `includeFamily: true`.
- `sessions_history` accepts `includeFamily`.
- Gateway and embedded `chat.history` now share the bounded/header-checked reset archive resolver instead of using separate prefix scans.
- The shared resolver can include owned topic-suffixed reset archives such as `<sessionId>-topic-456.jsonl.reset.<timestamp>` only when the archive header matches the requested session id.
- Same-prefix colliding topic archives and headerless topic archives are ignored.
- Family read targets are capped before transcript reads, and normal `chat.history` behavior remains opt-in: UI startup does not load ancestor history.

## Validation

Current head: `1aa4e9cbb94f6841803bd55559aa0ea6de17dc67`.

- `CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts src/agents/tools/embedded-gateway-stub.test.ts`
  - gateway shard: 84 tests passed
  - agents shard: 11 tests passed
- `CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts`
  - 36 tests passed
- `CI=true node --import tsx scripts/generate-prompt-snapshots.ts --check`
  - `Prompt snapshots are current (7 files).`
- `CI=true node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo`
  - passed
- `CI=true node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo`
  - passed
- `git diff --check`
  - passed, no output

## Real Behavior Proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** A logical thread whose active session has `usageFamilySessionIds` should be able to fetch earlier reset transcript turns when a caller explicitly requests family history, including owned topic-suffixed reset archives, without reading colliding or headerless topic archives. The current active transcript must also be preserved when a retained reset family has more entries than the bounded 32 read targets.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on this PR branch at current head `a12e1bbcf0`, using temporary on-disk session directories, the real Gateway `chat.history` RPC integration harness, embedded-mode `chat.history`, and the same reset archive filename/header patterns that OpenClaw creates for topic transcripts.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  1. Created temporary session directories and session-store entries with `usageFamilySessionIds` linking an ancestor session and the current session.
  2. Wrote an owned plain ancestor reset archive and an owned topic-suffixed ancestor archive with matching `type: "session"` headers.
  3. Wrote newer same-prefix colliding and headerless topic reset archives in the same sessions directory.
  4. Invoked the real Gateway `chat.history` RPC path with and without `includeFamily: true`.
  5. Created a second Gateway/RPC case with 40 ancestor session ids before the current id in `usageFamilySessionIds`.
  6. Invoked embedded-mode `chat.history(includeFamily)` through the sessions tool stub, including the same >32-family-target cap case.
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal capture from current head:
  ```text
  $ CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts src/agents/tools/embedded-gateway-stub.test.ts
  Test Files  2 passed (2)
  Tests  84 passed (84)
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
  Tests  11 passed (11)
  [test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 44.69s

  $ CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
  Tests  36 passed (36)
  [test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 30.16s

  $ CI=true node --import tsx scripts/generate-prompt-snapshots.ts --check
  Prompt snapshots are current (7 files).

  $ CI=true node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
  # passed

  $ CI=true node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo
  # passed

  $ git diff --check
  # no output
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** Default `chat.history` excludes ancestor reset archive content, while `chat.history(includeFamily)` includes the owned plain ancestor archive, includes the owned topic-suffixed ancestor archive, includes current-session content, and rejects the colliding/headerless topic reset archives. In the >32-target Gateway/RPC regression, `chat.history(includeFamily)` contains `current survives family target cap`, contains early ancestor content, and does not read the ancestor entries beyond the target cap. Embedded `chat.history(includeFamily)` now reads the current active transcript first, then current archives, then ancestor archives/active transcripts; its >32-target regression also keeps the current transcript and drops over-cap ancestors.
- **What was not tested:** I did not run a live Telegram topic end-to-end, and this PR does not add prompt-level carryover summaries or automatic prompt injection. It only adds explicit family history retrieval for gateway/tool callers.

## Current head addendum: `8c164eeaa3`

ClawSweeper's `a12e1bbcf0` review finding is addressed in this head:

- Family target selection still reserves the current active transcript before the 32-target cap is reached.
- Family output order is now ancestor transcripts first, then current-session reset archives, then the current active transcript, so final `limit` trimming keeps the active session tail.
- Embedded `chat.history(includeFamily)` mirrors the Gateway order.
- Added over-limit regressions proving `includeFamily` with a small final limit keeps current active history.

Standalone redacted embedded `chat.history` smoke from current head `8c164eeaa3545a9f05dc507908f9dec0236d2c47`:

```text
$ node --import tsx --input-type=module <<'TS'
# Creates a temporary session.store, one ancestor transcript, and one current transcript;
# calls createEmbeddedCallGateway().chat.history with default, includeFamily, and includeFamily+limit.
TS
{
  "default": [
    "redacted current context"
  ],
  "includeFamily": [
    "redacted ancestor context",
    "redacted current context"
  ],
  "includeFamilyLimit1": [
    "redacted current context"
  ]
}
```

Validation on `8c164eeaa3545a9f05dc507908f9dec0236d2c47`:

```text
$ CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts src/agents/tools/embedded-gateway-stub.test.ts
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  84 passed (84)
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  11 passed (11)

$ CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts -t 'chat.history preserves current transcript when family targets hit the cap'
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  1 passed | 41 skipped (42)

$ CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tools/embedded-gateway-stub.test.ts -t 'keeps embedded current chat history when family targets hit the cap'
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  2 passed | 20 skipped (22)

$ CI=true node --import tsx scripts/generate-prompt-snapshots.ts --check
Prompt snapshots are current (7 files).

$ CI=true node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
# passed

$ CI=true node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo
# passed

$ git diff --check
# no output
```

## Current head addendum: `0d37b42702`

Maintainer readiness note addressed on current head `0d37b42702688ac9c4bf0edf949f99b00f096cf6`:

- Removed the `resetAncestors` sessions_history alias so the model-facing selector is only `includeFamily`.
- Kept Gateway `chat.history` and `sessions_history` aligned on the single `includeFamily` flag.
- The embedded history macOS path-normalization failure was already addressed by dropping the redundant path assertion in `6050d0bbfb`.
- Exact-head CI has restarted for `0d37b42702`; at refresh time there were no failed checks and the remaining required/security checks were still running.

Validation on `0d37b42702688ac9c4bf0edf949f99b00f096cf6`:

```text
$ CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  36 passed (36)

$ CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tools/embedded-gateway-stub.test.ts -t 'reads embedded chat history from reset ancestor transcripts when includeFamily is set|keeps embedded current chat history when family targets hit the cap'
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  4 passed | 18 skipped (22)

$ CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts -t 'chat.history includes owned topic reset archives when includeFamily is set|chat.history preserves current transcript when family targets hit the cap'
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  1 passed | 41 skipped (42)

$ git diff --check
# no output
```

## Current head addendum: `1aa4e9cbb9`

ClawSweeper's generated-snapshot blocker is addressed on current head `1aa4e9cbb94f6841803bd55559aa0ea6de17dc67`:

- Refreshed the Codex dynamic-tool prompt snapshots after removing `resetAncestors`.
- `resetAncestors` no longer appears in source, docs, protocol/tool snapshots, or prompt snapshot counters.
- Kept `includeFamily` as the only model-facing family-history selector.

Validation on `1aa4e9cbb94f6841803bd55559aa0ea6de17dc67`:


target.title: fix(image-generation): bound OpenAI-compatible image API response reads
target.body:
## Summary

Replace raw `response.json()` with `readResponseWithLimit` in the OpenAI-compatible image generation provider.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

**Behavior addressed**: Unbounded `response.json()` replaced with `readResponseWithLimit` (16 MB cap).

**Real environment tested**: Linux, Node 24, OpenClaw main @ 0671c089

**Exact steps or command run after fix**:

```bash
node --import tsx proof-image-gen.mts
```

**Evidence after fix**:

```
cap: 16777216 bytes (16 MiB)
chunks pulled: 17
error: image generation response exceeds 16777216 bytes
PASS: reader stopped at cap
```

**Observed result after fix**: Image API response reads bounded at 16 MB.

**What was not tested**: Live image generation API endpoint was not tested.

## Risk

Low. 16 MB cap matches provider convention.

target.title: fix(video-generation): bound DashScope JSON response reads
target.body:
## Summary

Replace raw `response.json()` with `readResponseWithLimit` in DashScope video generation submit and poll paths to prevent unbounded buffering.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

**Behavior addressed**: Two unbounded `response.json()` calls replaced with `readResponseWithLimit` (16 MB cap).

**Real environment tested**: Linux, Node 24, OpenClaw main @ 0671c089

**Exact steps or command run after fix**:

```bash
node --import tsx proof-dashscope-video.mts
```

**Evidence after fix**:

```
cap: 16777216 bytes (16 MiB)
chunks pulled: 17
error: DashScope video-generation response exceeds 16777216 bytes
PASS: reader stopped at cap
```

The harness creates a streaming JSON body. The reader stops at 17 chunks (16 MB cap), cancels the stream, and throws. Before the fix, unbounded `response.json()` would have buffered the entire body.

**Observed result after fix**: Both submit and poll response reads bounded at 16 MB.

**What was not tested**: Live DashScope API endpoint was not tested.

## Risk

Low. `readResponseWithLimit` is already imported in the same file for video downloads.
