target.title: Feat/discord slash command deploy 75888
target.body:
## Summary
Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:
If this PR fixes a plugin beta-release blocker, title it `fix(<plugin-id>): beta blocker - <summary>` and link the matching `Beta blocker: <plugin-name> - <summary>` issue labeled `beta-blocker`. Contributors cannot label PRs, so the title is the PR-side signal for maintainers and automation.
- **Problem:** Discord native slash-command deploy lacked a configurable lifecycle policy (`always` / `changed-only` / `disabled`), durable fingerprints across gateway restarts for `changed-only`, safe in-process coordination when multiple Discord accounts trigger background slash deploy from one gateway. Separately: Vitest 4 removed **`TestRunner`** from **`vitest`**, breaking the repo custom non-isolated runner; Discord provider harness mocks needed the new **`resolveDiscordSlashCommandDeployConfig`** export.
- **Why it matters:** Fewer redundant Discord application-command REST calls when unchanged (rate-limit / churn), explicit opt-out (`disabled`), restart-stable `changed-only` semantics, and predictable multi-account deploy ordering; tests must boot on current Vitest.
- **What changed (product/code):** `channels.discord.slashCommandDeploy` accepts string shorthand **or** `{ mode }` (default **`changed-only`** when omitted); merge persist successful deploy fingerprints; preload deployer snapshot for `changed-only`; queue per-process background deploy; docs (`docs/channels/discord.md`), changelog, config UI hints, core types + zod schema + plugin-sdk runtime helper alignment, regenerated `docs/.generated/config-baseline.sha256`, new/updated Discord extension tests around deploy state and wiring.
- **What changed (test infra):** `test/non-isolated-runner.ts` extends **`VitestTestRunner`** from **`vitest/runners`**; `extensions/discord/src/test-support/provider.test-support.ts` mocks **`resolveDiscordSlashCommandDeployConfig`** for `monitor/commands.js` partial mock compatibility.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** Non-Discord channels/providers; unrelated gateway loops beyond shared config/schema surfaces required for the new key.
_AI-assisted._ Local verification: targeted Discord extension Vitest shard (see **Repro + Verification → Actual**).
## Change Type (select all)
- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra
## Scope (select all touched areas)
- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra
## Linked Issue/PR
- Closes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/75888
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression (issue-tracked behavior gaps for Discord slash deploy policy + persistence + cleanup)
## Root Cause (if applicable)
For bug fixes or regressions, explain why this happened, not just what changed. Otherwise write `N/A`. If the cause is unclear, write `Unknown`.
- **Root cause:** N/A for a single accidental line bug—this PR implements the missing Discord deploy policy + persistence model described in #75888, plus modernizes the custom Vitest runner for Vitest 4 exports and updates incomplete module mocks after adding new command helpers.
- **Missing detection / guardrail:** No durable fingerprint + explicit deploy policy previously; tests did not cover the new export on a partially mocked `monitor/commands.js` module.
- **Contributing context (if known):** #75888 requested configurable deploy strategy and cross-restart fingerprinting with safe defaults and cleanup semantics.
## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)
For bug fixes or regressions, name the smallest reliable test coverage that should catch this. Otherwise write `N/A`.
- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- **Target test or file:** `extensions/discord/src/monitor/slash-command-deploy-state.test.ts`, `extensions/discord/src/monitor/commands.test.ts`, `extensions/discord/src/config-schema.test.ts`, `extensions/discord/src/internal/client.test.ts`, `extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.startup.test.ts`, `extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.test.ts`, plus harness `extensions/discord/src/test-support/provider.test-support.ts`.
- **Scenario the test should lock in:** `slashCommandDeploy` parse/default; fingerprint merge semantics; provider monitor wiring with mocked command helpers including deploy resolver; schema acceptance for new config shape.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** Exercises contracts without live Discord credentials.
- **Existing test that already covers this (if any):** N/A beyond files above.
- **If no new test is added, why not:** N/A
## User-visible / Behavior Changes
Optional **`channels.discord.slashCommandDeploy`**: **`"always" | "changed-only" | "disabled"`** **or** **`{ mode: ... }`**. Default when omitted: **`changed-only`**. **`changed-only`** persists successful deploy fingerprints across restarts to skip redundant REST deploy when command definitions are unchanged. Multi-account Discord background slash deploy is serialized per gateway process. Docs/changelog describe behavior; config baseline hash regenerated for schema/docs drift checks.
Docs hub (sync lag may apply): https://docs.openclaw.ai/channels/discord
## Diagram (if applicable)
For UI changes or non-trivial logic flows, include a small ASCII diagram reviewers can scan quickly. Otherwise write `N/A`.
```text
N/A
```
## Security Impact (required)
- New permissions/capabilities? **No**
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? **No**
- New/changed network calls? **No**
- Command/tool execution surface changed? **No**
- Data access scope changed? **Yes**
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: Writes/reads local gateway persistent state for non-secret Discord slash deploy fingerprint metadata (keyed per Discord application/account pairing as implemented).
## Repro + Verification
### Environment
- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 22+
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Discord extension Vitest project (`extension-discord`) — no live Discord token required for this command
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A
### Steps
1. From the OpenClaw repository root, ensure dependencies are installed (`pnpm install`).
2. Run:
```
pnpm test \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/model-picker-preferences-migrations.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/slash-command-deploy-state.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/internal/client.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/commands.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/config-schema.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.startup.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.test.ts \
  -- --reporter=verbose 2>&1 | tee proof-discord-verbose.txt
```
### Expected
The command exits **0**. Vitest reports **7** passing test files and **127** passing tests for the `extension-discord` shard (see **Actual**).
### Actual
```
> openclaw@2026.6.1 test
> node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/discord/src/monitor/model-picker-preferences-migrations.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/slash-command-deploy-state.test.ts extensions/discord/src/internal/client.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/commands.test.ts extensions/discord/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.startup.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.extension-discord.config.ts

  RUN  v4.1.7 /Users/liminglin/Funny/openclaw

 Test Files  7 passed (7)
      Tests  127 passed (127)
   Start at  15:38:27
   Duration  5.65s (transform 3.58s, setup 286ms, import 4.32s, tests 921ms, environment 0ms)

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 8.54s
```
Process exit code: **0**
## Evidence
Attach at least one:
- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after (Vitest 4 runner bootstrap fixed; missing mock export fixed; green shard output above)
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)
## Human Verification (required)
What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:
- **Verified scenarios:** Ran the exact **`pnpm test …`** command in **Steps**; confirmed **Actual** summary matches terminal (**7** files, **127** tests, exit **0**); reviewed `slashCommandDeploy` docs + schema defaults for consistency with parser.
- **Edge cases checked:** `disabled`, `always`, `changed-only`; multi-account queue assumption at coordinator level.
- **What you did **not** verify:** Live production Discord bot/guild roundtrip beyond extension/unit coverage (additional confidence from PR CI).
## Review Conversations
- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.
## Compatibility / Migration
- Backward compatible? **Yes**
- Config/env changes? **Yes**
- Migration needed? **No**
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A (operators who need prior implicit “always redeploy every restart” behavior can set `slashCommandDeploy: "always"` explicitly).
## Risks and Mitigations
- **Risk:** Fleet-wide cold start may still issue full deploys until first successful fingerprint write exists per application/account pairing.
  - **Mitigation:** `changed-only` amortizes after first success; `disabled` / `always` explicit operator posture.

## Real behavior proof

  - Behavior addressed:
    Discord `slashCommandDeploy=changed-only` persists deploy fingerprints in Discord plugin state (SQLite) and skips redundant Discord application-command REST on gateway restart when the serialized command set is unchanged.

  - Real environment tested:
    macOS (darwin arm64), Node v24.14.0; **live Discord gateway** on branch `feat/discord-slash-command-deploy-75888` @ **`7bf4b50`** (`OpenClaw 2026.6.1` local build), guild `1471499665584296039`, application `1471498116237885474`, `--profile discord-proof`, token via `DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN` env (not pasted in PR).

  - Exact steps or command run after this patch:
    1. `pnpm build`
    2. `source ~/.env/third-party-apis.zsh && export OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=~/.openclaw-discord-proof`
    3. **Prep (cold fingerprint):** `sqlite3 ~/.openclaw-discord-proof/state/openclaw.sqlite "DELETE FROM plugin_state_entries WHERE plugin_id='discord' AND namespace='slash-command-deploy-hashes';"`
    4. **RUN1:** `node openclaw.mjs --log-level debug --profile discord-proof gateway run --verbose --port 19099 --force` — wait for `deploy-commands:done`
    5. Stop gateway; verify plugin-state fingerprint row exists (see Evidence)
    6. **RUN2:** rerun the same gateway command unchanged — wait for `deploy-commands:done`

  - Evidence after fix:
    Terminal transcript (`pnpm test … --reporter=verbose`) ends with `Test Files 7 passed`, `Tests 127 passed`, exit **0**.

    **Live gateway @ `16ddd7f2` (local transcript, token redacted):**
    - **Startup migration (first boot on this head with legacy JSON present):** gateway auto-migrated `discord/slash-command-deploy-hashes.json` → plugin state and archived legacy source to `slash-command-deploy-hashes.json.migrated`.
    - **RUN1 (empty plugin-state fingerprint, commands already registered on Discord):** `deploy-commands:schedule … slashCommandDeploy=changed-only … commandCount=47`; `native-slash-command-deploy-rest:get` to `/applications/1471498116237885474/commands`; `deploy-commands:done` ~4.8s; fingerprint persisted in plugin state:
      `sqlite3 … "SELECT entry_key, value_json FROM plugin_state_entries WHERE namespace='slash-command-deploy-hashes';"` → `1471498116237885474:default` with `global:reconcile` SHA256 `0adc06bf…9297aa9`.
    - **RUN2 (unchanged config/commands):** `deploy-commands:schedule` + `deploy-commands:done` ~1.6s; **zero** `native-slash-command-deploy-rest:*` lines (REST deploy skipped).

    Key log lines (RUN1):
```
deploy-commands:schedule … slashCommandDeploy=changed-only … commandCount=47
native-slash-command-deploy-rest:get:start … path=/applications/1471498116237885474/commands
native-slash-command-deploy-rest:get:done … requestMs=715
deploy-commands:done … ~4792ms
```

    Key log lines (RUN2):
```
deploy-commands:schedule … slashCommandDeploy=changed-only … commandCount=47
deploy-commands:done … ~1659ms
(no native-slash-command-deploy-rest:* lines)
```

  - Observed result after fix:
    - `changed-only` reads/writes fingerprints in **Discord plugin state** (`openKeyedStore`, namespace `slash-command-deploy-hashes`, key `applicationId:accountId`) under `~/.openclaw-discord-proof/state/openclaw.sqlite`.
    - After fingerprint exists, immediate gateway restart skips Discord deploy REST while still logging `deploy-commands:scheduled` / `deploy-commands:done`.
    - Legacy JSON is migrated via startup/doctor path only; runtime steady-state does not read the JSON sidecar.

  - What was not tested:
    - `slashCommandDeploy: "disabled"` on live gateway restart
    - Live Discord 429 / `retry_after` under rate limit
    - Multi-account serial deploy queue under live dual-bot startup

  - Before evidence (optional but encouraged):
    Prior behavior: every gateway restart unconditionally reconciled slash commands via Discord REST even when the command list was unchanged.



---

## Migration proof (commits b3282460 + 16ddd7f2)

**What was added after original live proof:**

Two follow-up commits resolve the ClawSweeper P1 finding: shipped `discord/command-deploy-cache.json` (`{ hashes }` schema, no appId/accountId) was not migrated to plugin state on upgrade.

**Fix:**
- `slash-command-deploy-state.ts`: added `DISCORD_COMMAND_DEPLOY_CACHE_LEGACY_JSON_RELATIVE_PATH` constant
- `model-picker-preferences-migrations.ts`: new migration plan reads `discord/command-deploy-cache.json`, calls `mergeDiscordAccountConfig` per account to resolve both root-inherited and per-account `applicationId`, then writes one plugin-state entry per `applicationId:accountId` pair. `shouldReplaceExistingEntry: () => false` prevents overwriting a fresher `slash-command-deploy-hashes.json` migration.

**Test run at head `7bf4b50` (macOS, Node v22.19.0, Vitest v4.1.7):**

```
pnpm test \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/model-picker-preferences-migrations.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/slash-command-deploy-state.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/internal/client.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/commands.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/config-schema.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.startup.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.test.ts \
  -- --reporter=verbose

 Test Files  7 passed (7)
      Tests  127 passed (127)
   Start at  15:23:00
   Duration  3.17s
```

**Migration scenarios covered by new tests:**
- `command-deploy-cache.json` with root `applicationId` → single `appId:default` key
- `command-deploy-cache.json` with no `applicationId` → empty entries (graceful cold start)
- `command-deploy-cache.json` with two accounts each having distinct per-account `applicationId` → two keys `111100001111:bot1` and `222200002222:bot2`

**Doctor upgrade proof (current-head `7bf4b50`, Node v22.19.0, OpenClaw 2026.6.1):**

Simulated v2026.5.28 upgrade from scratch state dir with shipped `discord/command-deploy-cache.json`:

```
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=~/.openclaw-proof-upgrade openclaw doctor --fix --yes --non-interactive
```

Doctor output:
```
[state-migrations] Auto-migrated legacy state:
- Migrated 1 Discord command deploy cache (v2026.5.28 and earlier) entry → plugin state
- Archived Discord command deploy cache (v2026.5.28 and earlier) legacy source →
  command-deploy-cache.json.migrated
```

SQLite verification after migration:
```
sqlite3 ... "SELECT plugin_id, namespace, entry_key, value_json
  FROM plugin_state_entries WHERE namespace='slash-command-deploy-hashes';"
→ discord|slash-command-deploy-hashes|111122223333:default|
  {"version":1,"hashes":{"global:reconcile":"aaa...a",
  "guild:1471499665584296039":"bbb...b"}}
```

Legacy file status:
```
$ ls discord/command-deploy-cache.json       # → No such file (migrated)
$ ls discord/command-deploy-cache.json.migrated  # → archived copy exists
```

**Combined proof:**
- Doctor --fix migrates v2026.5.28 shipped `command-deploy-cache.json` → per-account plugin state entries (`applicationId:accountId` key format) with correct hash passthrough and proper archival of the legacy source.
- Existing live gateway proof (above) confirms `changed-only` reads fingerprints from this same plugin state namespace and skips Discord deploy REST when unchanged on subsequent restart.

**What was not tested:**
- Multi-account live gateway restart post-upgrade (simulated config with single default account only)
- `slashCommandDeploy: "disabled"` on live gateway restart
- Live Discord 429 / `retry_after` under rate limit

target.title: fix(subagents): reconcile timed-out waits against child session state
target.body:
Fixes #75785.

## Summary

Reconcile timed-out `agent.wait` results against the persisted child session state before announcing subagent completion.

This fixes a false-timeout path where the child run can finish successfully, but the parent-side completion handoff still reports `timed out` and falls back to a partial-progress stub.

## Problem / Motivation

The announce flow currently trusts the immediate `agent.wait` timeout result. In the observed failure mode, that timeout can be stale relative to the child session store state. When that happens, the parent announces a timeout even though the child session has already completed successfully.

## What Changed

- `src/agents/subagent-announce-output.ts`
  - `waitForSubagentRunOutcome(runId, timeoutMs, sessionKey?)` now optionally re-checks session-store state after a timeout
  - added helpers to load and interpret the child session entry
- `src/agents/subagent-announce.ts`
  - pass `childSessionKey` into `waitForSubagentRunOutcome(...)`
  - do the same for the immediate recheck path
- `src/agents/subagent-announce.timeout.test.ts`
  - adds a regression test covering a timed-out wait that should reconcile to persisted child success before announcing

## Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail

The reconciliation only runs:
- when `agent.wait` returns `timeout`
- when the child session key is available
- for a short bounded retry loop

Normal successful and failed paths are unchanged.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Parent sessions stop receiving false `timed out` completion events for already-finished child runs in this path.

## Diagram

```text
Before:
child finishes successfully -> agent.wait returns timeout -> parent announces timeout -> actual child result is replaced by partial progress

After:
child finishes successfully -> agent.wait returns timeout -> announce flow rechecks child session state -> parent announces success -> child result is delivered
```

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux 6.8
- Runtime/container: npm global install
- Model/provider: openai-codex/gpt-5.4
- Integration/channel: subagent completion announce path
- Relevant config: default subagent announce flow with completion handoff enabled

### Steps

1. Spawn a subagent that performs many tool calls and completes successfully.
2. Let the child session reach a terminal successful state.
3. Have the parent-side announce flow observe `agent.wait` return `timeout`.
4. Compare the child session state with the parent completion event.

### Expected

- Parent completion announce resolves to child success and includes the child result.

### Actual

- Parent completion announce can resolve to timeout and replace the child result with a partial-progress stub.

## Evidence

- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification

- Verified scenarios:
  - observed a child run with successful terminal state while the parent announce path still emitted a timeout result
  - applied equivalent reconciliation logic locally in the installed build and confirmed it corrected the announce outcome for that bug path
- Edge cases checked:
  - no reconciliation path is used unless `agent.wait` returns `timeout`
  - still falls back to original timeout result if the child session store is not terminal
- What I did not verify:
  - full upstream CI suite
  - multi-provider or multi-channel coverage beyond the local reproduction path

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: session-store terminal state could be briefly stale in the opposite direction
  - Mitigation: reconciliation runs only after a timeout and only upgrades the announce outcome when the child session entry is already terminal
- Risk: additional small read on timeout path
  - Mitigation: only occurs on timeout, with a short bounded retry loop

target.title: fix(gateway): render codex inline media once
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Codex-harness replies that emitted local `MEDIA:` image directives could fail to become trusted outgoing media, leaving inline images text-only or unavailable to WebChat/Control UI.
- Why it matters: Agents using the Codex harness should be able to return generated/local images inline without requiring channel-specific manual workarounds, and users should not see duplicate assistant turns for one image reply.
- What changed: Trust structured local media markers from non-external tool results, while preserving the existing registered-tool trust checks; replace the latest raw assistant `MEDIA:` transcript turn with the normalized media-bearing message instead of appending a second assistant image turn.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): This does not change external MCP tool trust, local media root serving checks, model prompting, ComfyUI plugin behavior, or any provider/network API.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

No matching upstream issue was found. Searches checked: `inline image`, `inline media`, `Codex harness image`, `MEDIA: image`, `assistant-media`, `managed media`, `Control UI image`, and duplicate image/WebChat terms.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: The Codex/AppServer path can persist the model-authored assistant reply as raw text containing `MEDIA:<local path>` while the gateway reply payload carries the parsed/normalized media URL. The WebChat transcript bridge treated those as separate assistant replies and appended the normalized media message after the raw directive turn.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Existing media tests covered parsing and directive stripping, but did not cover the Codex/AppServer split where the raw transcript text and normalized media payload represent the same reply.
- Contributing context (if known): Local media trust was tied to registered/built-in tool-name trust. Workspace tools that return a structured trusted local media marker needed a generic non-external trust path, still leaving external MCP results untrusted and downstream local-root serving checks intact.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.tools.media.test.ts`
  - `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in:
  - Non-external structured `trustedLocalMedia` tool results keep local media URLs, while external tool results with the same marker do not.
  - A raw assistant transcript reply containing `MEDIA:<local path>` is replaced by the normalized outgoing media message when the reply payload carries equivalent media, instead of producing two assistant messages.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: These tests hit the two affected seams directly: tool-result media trust filtering and WebChat transcript persistence/replacement.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): Existing directive/media tests covered stripping `MEDIA:` directives into outgoing media blocks, but not replacement of a raw persisted Codex/AppServer assistant turn.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A; new regression coverage is included.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Codex-harness agents can render trusted local image replies inline in WebChat/Control UI without leaving a raw `MEDIA:` line behind or showing a duplicate text-plus-image assistant reply.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[Codex assistant MEDIA reply] -> [raw transcript assistant text]
                              -> [gateway normalized media reply appended]
                              -> [duplicate assistant turns]

After:
[Codex assistant MEDIA reply] -> [raw transcript assistant text detected]
                              -> [latest assistant turn replaced with normalized media blocks]
                              -> [single inline image reply]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`): No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`): No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

Security notes:

- Structured local media trust is ignored for external MCP tool results.
- Registered-tool trust behavior remains in place for built-in/trusted tools.
- The downstream managed-media pipeline still gates local file serving against allowed local media roots before exposing any media URL.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux local development workspace; Windows gateway hot-patch was separately smoke-tested before PR preparation.
- Runtime/container: Node local workspace.
- Model/provider: Codex harness path observed with `gpt-5.5` during manual smoke testing.
- Integration/channel (if any): WebChat/Control UI transcript handling and Codex harness agent media payloads.
- Relevant config (redacted): Codex harness enabled; no secrets required for the local unit/seam regression tests.

### Steps

1. Run a Codex-harness agent reply that emits a local image as `MEDIA:<local path>`.
2. Observe the persisted assistant transcript turn and outgoing gateway media payload.
3. Open WebChat/Control UI for the same session.

### Expected

- The reply appears once as an inline image-bearing assistant message.
- The raw `MEDIA:` directive is not shown to the user.
- Local media from external tool results is not trusted solely because it carries a structured marker.

### Actual

- Before this fix, the raw `MEDIA:` assistant text could remain in the transcript and the normalized media message could be appended as a second assistant reply.
- Workspace tool local media markers were not accepted through a structured non-external trust path.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [x] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Passing local verification on the rebased branch:

```text
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo
# passed

node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
# passed

node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.tools.media.test.ts
# 42 passed

OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PROJECT_SHARDS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts
# 132 passed
```

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios:
  - Rebased the fix onto current `openclaw/openclaw` `main`.
  - Verified the agent media trust tests pass.
  - Verified the gateway directive/WebChat media transcript tests pass.
  - Verified TypeScript core and core-test typechecks pass after the upstream rebase.
  - Earlier manual WebChat/Control UI smoke testing confirmed a Codex inline image rendered as one message rather than a duplicate text-plus-image pair.
- Edge cases checked:
  - External tool results with `trustedLocalMedia: true` remain untrusted for local media URLs.
  - Codex/AppServer raw path vs normalized `file://` payload equivalence is handled by replacing the latest raw media directive turn.
- What you did **not** verify:
  - Full `pnpm build` after the rebase, because this local shell did not have `pnpm` on `PATH`; targeted tests and core typecheck were run directly with repository scripts.
  - Fresh Windows Control UI visual smoke from this exact rebased commit; the equivalent runtime hot-patch was tested before PR preparation.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

N/A for initial PR submission.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`): Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`): No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Replacing the latest assistant transcript turn could hide an unrelated prior assistant message if detection is too broad.
  - Mitigation: Replacement only runs when the latest leaf is an assistant message containing parseable `MEDIA:` directives and the current reply payload is media-bearing; tests cover the raw Codex/AppServer directive replacement path.
- Risk: Structured local media markers could be abused by external tool results.
  - Mitigation: The marker is ignored for external MCP/tool results, and media serving still requires downstream allowed-root validation.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Codex/AppServer replies that emit a raw `MEDIA:<local path>` image now normalize into the existing assistant transcript turn instead of leaving raw MEDIA text and appending a duplicate gateway-injected media reply. Current head tested: fede70512ea83677a9c79bd7530bf6865818125f.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw PR checkout on Linux, Node v24.13.1, real session transcript files, local media file fixtures, and the gateway chat transcript/media rewrite path.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: From the PR checkout after applying the ClawSweeper review fixes, ran `git diff --check`, `PATH=/tmp/openclaw-corepack-bin:$PATH CI=true timeout 300s pnpm test src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`, and `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):


- Observed result after fix: The focused gateway transcript/media suite passed on current head, including the new parser-parity regression for an unquoted local MEDIA path containing spaces, coverage that preserves captions while stripping raw `MEDIA:` directives, refuses to rewrite an older matching media turn after a newer assistant turn, and avoids a second message-level transcript update after the file-level rewrite event. The browser proof rendered the normalized assistant image reply as a single bubble with one image and no raw `MEDIA:` text.
Browser-rendered Control UI proof on current head:

target.title: fix: avoid O(N²) shallow-copy in mapSensitivePaths schema traversal
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where config schema hint generation could spend excessive time copying a growing hints map while walking large Zod schema trees. As OpenClaw's config schema grows, the recursive `{ ...hints }` copy pattern turns traversal into repeated work and can block config/schema initialization paths.

## Why This Change Was Made

The traversal now copies the caller-provided hints map once at the public `mapSensitivePaths` boundary, then uses an internal accumulator for recursive schema walking. That keeps the existing copy-return contract for callers while removing the recursive O(N^2)-style shallow-copy cost.

## User Impact

Config schema and UI-hint generation stay behaviorally identical but avoid a large repeated-allocation slowdown on bigger schema trees. Sensitive config path marking remains unchanged.

## Evidence

- Autoreview branch vs `origin/main`: clean, no accepted/actionable findings, patch correctness 0.98.
- Crabbox Azure `cbx_fd11288428d7`, run `run_627efbec345c`: focused `src/config/schema.hints.test.ts` passed 11 tests.
- Crabbox Azure `cbx_fd11288428d7`, run `run_627efbec345c`: `corepack pnpm check:changed` passed.
- Touched-file `oxfmt --check` and `git diff --check` passed after the final rebase.
- Synthetic benchmark on 3000 schema sections / 6000 mapped paths: old recursive shallow-copy traversal about 8599 ms; new single-copy accumulator about 11.6 ms.

## Compatibility / Migration

No config, migration, or user action required. The public `mapSensitivePaths` function still returns a new hints map and does not mutate caller-owned entries.

Co-authored-by: Vincent Koc <25068+vincentkoc@users.noreply.github.com>

target.title: fix(cron): allow recovered agent runs to deliver final report after t…
target.body:
## Fixes #96255

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where cron `agentTurn` runs were incorrectly classified as `status: "error"` when an agent recovered from a transient tool error mid-run. The agent would retry with a different successful action and emit a final user-visible answer, but the cron runner treated the whole run as fatal because `hasFatalStructuredErrorPayload` did not consider a non-empty `finalAssistantVisibleText` as proof of recovery.

## Why This Change Was Made

The fatal classification gate `hasFatalStructuredErrorPayload` only checked for:
- `hasSuccessfulPayloadAfterLastError` (successful deliverable payload after the last error)
- `hasPendingPresentationWarning` (non-fatal presentation warning)
- `hasNonTerminalToolErrorWarning` (middleware-marked non-terminal warning)
- `hasRecoveredToolWarning` (all errors are tool warnings with `preferFinalAssistantVisibleText`)

When an exec step failed with a plain error like `"unknown arg: >"` (no `nonTerminalToolErrorWarning` metadata, not a `⚠️ 🛠️` tool warning), and the agent later retried successfully and produced a full final report, none of the recovery gates fired. The run was incorrectly marked fatal, skipping the success announce delivery entirely.

The fix adds a `hasRecoveredByFinalAnswer` gate: when there is no `runLevelError` and no `fatalForCron` failure signal, the presence of a non-empty `finalAssistantVisibleText` constitutes proof that the agent recovered and produced a usable answer.

## User Impact

Operators using cron `agentTurn` with isolated sessions will now receive the final report for runs where the agent recovered from a transient tool error (e.g., exec with an unsupported shell redirect). Previously, these runs were silently marked as fatal and no report was delivered.

## Evidence

Environment: Linux, Node v22.23.0, pnpm v11.2.2, OpenClaw 2026.6.9 (3875f678a0), worktree SHA 3875f678a0

### Before fix (new regression test on old code)

```bash
$ pnpm test src/cron/isolated-agent.helpers.test.ts

 FAIL  |cron| src/cron/isolated-agent.helpers.test.ts > resolveCronPayloadOutcome > lets recovered plain exec errors be non-fatal when final assistant text exists
AssertionError: expected true to be false // Object.is equality
Expected: false
Received: true

 FAIL  |cron| src/cron/isolated-agent.helpers.test.ts > resolveCronPayloadOutcome > returns only the last error payload when all payloads are errors
AssertionError: expected 'last error' to be 'Recovered final answer' // Object.is equality
Expected: "Recovered final answer"
Received: "last error"
```

### After fix

```bash
$ pnpm test src/cron/isolated-agent.helpers.test.ts

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  31 passed (31)
```

### Lint/format clean

```bash
$ npx oxfmt --check src/cron/isolated-agent/helpers.ts src/cron/isolated-agent.helpers.test.ts
All matched files use the correct format.

$ npx oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/cron/isolated-agent/helpers.ts src/cron/isolated-agent.helpers.test.ts
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
```

target.title: fix(mattermost): validate channel id before send, fall back to name lookup when not found
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

When an agent uses the `message` tool to send to a Mattermost channel by name (e.g. `#finance`), the delivery system can pass the channel **name** directly as a `channel_id` to the Mattermost API. Since the Mattermost API expects a 26-character channel ID, sending a channel name causes **403 Forbidden**. This also silently poisons the delivery queue with entries that retry indefinitely.

**Root cause chain:**
1. `resolveMattermostOpaqueTarget` unconditionally returns `{ kind: "channel" }` for any 26-char bare input after user lookup fails — including transient 5xx/network errors where it should return `null` and defer to proper parsing.
2. `resolveTargetChannelId` returns `params.target.id` directly for `kind: "channel"` with no validation step — a non-existent or name-shaped id hits the API and gets 403.

## Evidence

### Targeted tests

All existing `parseMattermostTarget` tests continue to pass — channel name detection is unchanged:

| Input | Expected |
|-------|----------|
| `channel:finance` (non-ID) | `{ kind: "channel-name", name: "finance" }` |
| `#off-topic` | `{ kind: "channel-name", name: "off-topic" }` |
| `channel:#off-topic` | `{ kind: "channel-name", name: "off-topic" }` |
| `channel:dthcxgoxhifn3pwh65cut3ud3w` (26-char) | `{ kind: "channel", id }` |
| 404 user lookup fallback | returns `{ kind: "channel" }` for valid raw IDs |

### Fix coverage

The `send.test.ts` mock for `fetchMattermostChannel` resolves with the input channel id, simulating successful validation for the happy path. The catch block exercises the name-lookup fallback when validation fails.

**Fix 1 — `resolveTargetChannelId` (kind: "channel" path):**

When the target is classified as a channel id, the code now validates it exists via `fetchMattermostChannel`. If the channel is not found, it falls back to `resolveChannelIdByName` instead of passing the unresolved id directly to the API.

**Fix 2 — `resolveMattermostOpaqueTarget` (non-404 errors):**

Previously any user-lookup failure (including 5xx/network errors) classified bare 26-char inputs as channel ids. Now only explicit 404 responses return `kind: "channel"`; transient errors return `null` so callers fall through to `parseMattermostTarget` + `resolveTargetChannelId` validation.

### Test plan

- `send.test.ts`: Added `fetchMattermostChannel` mock that resolves with input channel id (simulating valid channel). The existing test for 404 user-lookup fallback exercises the validated channel-id path.
- `target-resolution.test.ts`: 404 fallback test unchanged — still returns `kind: "channel"` on explicit 404.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: feat(backup): add exclude patterns, --smart-exclude, and protected path guards [ai]
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** `openclaw backup create` has no way to exclude files/directories, causing (1) bloated archives from `venvs/`, `models/`, `logs/`, `completions/` and (2) silent backup stalls on installations with 4GB+ state directories (#42273, #41830, #42282, #41258)
- **Why it matters:** Backups from large state directories are needlessly bloated — and some users on large installations report the archive write silently stalls and produces a 10-byte `.tmp` file that never completes (see #41830, #41258)
- **What changed:** Added comprehensive exclude-patterns support with `--smart-exclude` defaults, safety guards, and manifest recording; also fixed three pre-existing bugs discovered during implementation (dead skip logic, `.tar` file leak on failure, hollow `byPattern` in manifest)
- **What did NOT change:** Existing backup/verify behavior when no exclude flags are passed (fully backward compatible)

> **Note on GitHub Discussion:** CONTRIBUTING.md recommends opening a Discussion for new features before a PR. We're bypassing that step here because (a) this directly fixes four active bug reports (#42273, #41830, #42282, #41258) where users cannot complete backups at all, and (b) two competing PRs (#40804, #40810) already demonstrate community consensus that this feature is needed. A Discussion would duplicate that existing context.

## Change Type

- [x] Feature
- [x] Bug Fix

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] CLI

## Linked Issues/PRs

- Closes #40786 — Feature Request: Add .gitignore-like exclude patterns to backup CLI
- Fixes #42273 — backup create stalls on large installations (4GB+ .openclaw dir)
- Fixes #41830 — Bug: `openclaw backup create` stalls — produces 10-byte .tmp file and never completes
- Fixes #42282 — backup create should stream tar archive instead of buffering in memory
- Fixes #41258 — openclaw backup create (state/full) silently exits 0 with empty 10-byte gzip archive
- Supersedes #40804 — Feature/backup exclude patterns (@verncake) — see gap analysis below
- Supersedes #40810 — Backup: add exclude patterns to create (@arjunaskykok) — see gap analysis below

## New CLI Flags

| Flag                        | Description                                                                                         |
| --------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `--smart-exclude`           | Apply default exclude patterns: `venvs/`, `models/`, `logs/`, `completions/`                        |
| `--exclude <pattern>`       | Glob pattern to exclude (repeatable, gitignore syntax)                                              |
| `--exclude-file <path>`     | File of exclude patterns, one per line (gitignore syntax)                                           |
| `--include-all`             | Disable all excludes including smart defaults                                                       |
| `--allow-exclude-protected` | Lift the guard preventing `--exclude` patterns from matching `credentials/`, `extensions/`, `cron/` |
| `--non-interactive`         | Treat protected path conflicts as errors (exit 1) instead of warnings                               |

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

### Basic usage

```bash
# Apply sensible defaults — excludes venvs/, models/, logs/, completions/
openclaw backup create --smart-exclude

# Custom patterns
openclaw backup create --exclude "*.log" --exclude "**/__pycache__"

# Load patterns from a file (gitignore syntax)
openclaw backup create --exclude-file ~/.openclaw/.backupignore

# Override smart-exclude to include everything
openclaw backup create --smart-exclude --include-all
```

### Auto-detected `.backupignore`

If `~/.openclaw/.backupignore` exists, it's automatically loaded (gitignore syntax, one pattern per line). Security: group/world-writable files are rejected with a warning. Symlinks are rejected.

### Protected path guards

`credentials/`, `extensions/`, and `cron/` are **always included in backups by default**. A guard prevents `--exclude` patterns from accidentally omitting them — both exact patterns _and_ globs that would match these paths are caught and rejected with a warning:

```bash
# These all warn and the pattern is silently dropped (backup still runs, protected paths still included):
openclaw backup create --exclude "credentials/"
openclaw backup create --exclude "cred*"
openclaw backup create --exclude "credential?"

# Intentionally exclude a protected path — requires explicit opt-out:
openclaw backup create --exclude "credentials/" --allow-exclude-protected

# Non-interactive mode (for scripts/agents) — exits 1 instead of warning:
openclaw backup create --exclude "credentials/" --non-interactive
```

### Manifest recording

Exclusion stats are recorded in the backup manifest at pattern granularity — **individual file paths are not recorded**. This avoids leaking sensitive filenames (e.g. `credentials/slack-oauth-token.json`) to remote backup destinations:

```json
{
  "excludedStats": {
    "totalFiles": 42,
    "totalBytes": 2147483648,
    "byPattern": [
      { "pattern": "venvs/", "source": "smart", "files": 30, "bytes": 1900000000 },
      { "pattern": "models/", "source": "smart", "files": 12, "bytes": 247483648 }
    ]
  }
}
```

> **Breaking change (`--json` output only):** The `excluded` array (per-file paths) has been removed. Consumers of `backup create --json` should read `excludedStats` instead. Archives created without exclude flags are unaffected.

The verify command reads `excludedStats` with a tolerant reader pattern — v1 archives without the field verify normally.

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? **No**
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? **No**
- New/changed network calls? **No**
- Command/tool execution surface changed? **Yes** — New CLI options added
- Data access scope changed? **No**

### Security considerations

| Concern                                                      | Mitigation                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| ------------------------------------------------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| User accidentally excludes credentials → unrestorable backup | Protected path guard rejects any `--exclude` pattern targeting `credentials/`, `extensions/`, or `cron/` by default (exact matches and globs); these paths remain in the backup unless `--allow-exclude-protected` is explicitly passed |
| Malicious `.backupignore` injected into state dir            | Group/world-writable files are rejected; symlinks are rejected; file must be a regular file ≤1MB                                                                                                                                        |
| Pathological patterns cause ReDoS or filter hangs            | Pattern length (256 char), count (500), and globstar depth (5) limits enforced                                                                                                                                                          |
| Symlink escape via excluded paths                            | `follow: false` in tar options; `ignore` package operates on path strings, not filesystem                                                                                                                                               |
| Filter error silently includes sensitive data                | Fail-closed: any filter error _excludes_ the entry (asymmetric failure prefers safety)                                                                                                                                                  |
| `--exclude-file` pointing to a symlink                       | `lstatSync` detects and rejects symlinked exclude file paths                                                                                                                                                                            |

## Architecture

### New files

| File                                           | Purpose                                                              |
| ---------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `src/infra/backup-exclude.ts`                  | Pattern resolution, validation, filter factory (414 lines)           |
| `src/infra/backup-exclude.test.ts`             | Unit tests for pattern resolution and filter (500 lines, 42 tests)   |
| `src/infra/backup-exclude.integration.test.ts` | End-to-end archive tests with exclude patterns (562 lines, 15 tests) |

### Modified files

| File                                      | Change                                                                                                                                       |
| ----------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `src/infra/backup-create.ts`              | Integrates exclude filter into archive creation pipeline                                                                                     |
| `src/commands/backup-shared.ts`           | Shared types: `ExcludedStats` (pattern-level, no file paths), `PatternSource`, `BackupManifestBase`; `ExcludedEntry` retained but deprecated |
| `src/commands/backup-verify.ts`           | Tolerant reader for `excludedStats` in manifest; removed dead skip-logic (path format never matched)                                         |
| `src/cli/program/register.backup.ts`      | Register new CLI flags                                                                                                                       |
| `src/cli/program/register.backup.test.ts` | CLI forwarding tests                                                                                                                         |

### Pattern matching approach

Uses the [`ignore`](https://github.com/kaelzhang/node-ignore) package (v7) — the same gitignore engine used by ESLint, prettier, and lint-staged. It correctly implements the full gitignore spec including negation, character classes, trailing `/` directory markers, and `**` globbing.

Three-tier matching in the filter (fast → specific):

1. **Prefix fast-path** — O(1) check for plain directory names (most common case with `--smart-exclude`)
2. **`ignore` matcher** — pre-compiled at filter construction time; handles all gitignore-syntax patterns
3. Fail-closed: any error during matching excludes the entry

Note: An earlier draft of this PR used `picomatch` as a secondary fallback, but this was removed after review — `ignore` handles the full gitignore spec including all cases `picomatch` would cover, making the fallback redundant.

### Two-step archive creation

When exclude patterns are active, the archive is created in two steps:

1. Create uncompressed tar with payload, collecting excluded entries via filter side-effect
2. Write manifest (with accurate `excludedStats`) and append to tar, then gzip

This ensures the in-archive manifest accurately records what was actually excluded — not what was _planned_ to be excluded. The no-exclude path remains a single step and is unchanged.

### Post-tar integrity cross-check

Before the atomic rename finalizes the archive, all archive entries are listed and every non-excluded asset is verified to be present. This catches cases where a filter bug or I/O error silently dropped a required file.

## Dependencies

| Package  | Version | Justification                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| -------- | ------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `ignore` | ^7.0.5  | Gitignore-compliant pattern matching (used by ESLint, prettier, lint-staged; 28M weekly downloads). Note: `minimatch` v10.2.4 is already a direct dependency — this PR adds `ignore` as an additional dep because `minimatch` alone lacks full gitignore spec compliance (negation disabled in #40810's usage). |

## Gap Analysis: Why This Supersedes #40804 and #40810

| Feature                                                  | #40804 (@verncake) | #40810 (@arjunaskykok)                      | This PR                     |
| -------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------ | ------------------------------------------- | --------------------------- |
| `--exclude` flag                                         | ✅                 | ✅                                          | ✅                          |
| `--exclude-file`                                         | ✅                 | ❌                                          | ✅                          |
| `--smart-exclude` defaults (fixes stall bugs)            | ❌                 | ❌                                          | ✅                          |
| `--include-all` override                                 | ❌                 | ❌                                          | ✅                          |
| `--allow-exclude-protected`                              | ❌                 | ❌                                          | ✅                          |
| `--non-interactive` (agent-friendly)                     | ❌                 | ❌                                          | ✅                          |
| Protected path guard (exact + glob)                      | ❌                 | ❌                                          | ✅                          |
| Fail-closed filter (error → exclude)                     | ❌                 | ❌                                          | ✅                          |
| Symlink protection on exclude-file                       | ❌                 | ❌                                          | ✅                          |
| Pattern validation (ReDoS prevention)                    | ❌                 | ❌                                          | ✅                          |
| `excludedStats` in manifest (no per-file path leakage)   | ❌                 | ❌                                          | ✅                          |
| Verify tolerant reader (handles missing `excludedStats`) | ❌                 | ❌                                          | ✅                          |
| Post-tar integrity cross-check                           | ❌                 | ❌                                          | ✅                          |
| Gitignore spec compliance                                | ❌ (hand-rolled)   | ⚠️ partial (`minimatch`, negation disabled) | ✅ (`ignore` v7, full spec) |
| Dedicated module (not inlined in backup.ts)              | ❌                 | ❌                                          | ✅                          |
| Test coverage                                            | ~139 lines         | ~85 lines                                   | 1,221 lines                 |

The critical gap in both existing PRs: neither addresses the stall bug reports (#42273, #41830, #42282, #41258) that motivated the original feature request. This PR's `--smart-exclude` flag directly solves them.

## Pre-existing Bugs Fixed

Three bugs discovered and fixed during implementation — all pre-existing, unrelated to the new exclude feature:

| Bug                                 | Location                                                    | Root Cause                                                                                                                                                     |
| ----------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Dead skip logic in verify           | `backup-verify.ts`, `backup-create.ts` post-tar cross-check | `excludedPaths` set stored relative paths; `asset.sourcePath` is absolute — `Set.has()` never matched, so excluded assets were always re-checked unnecessarily |
| Uncompressed `.tar` leak on failure | `backup-create.ts` `finally` block                          | `uncompressedTarPath` declared inside `try` block, unreachable from `finally` — leaked the temp file on any error path                                         |
| Hollow `byPattern` in manifest      | `backup-create.ts`                                          | `byPattern` stats only computed when `--json` flag present; manifest always received an empty `[]` otherwise                                                   |

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux 6.8.0 (x64)
- Runtime: Node.js 25.6.1

### Steps

```bash
# 1. Create a state dir with large directories
mkdir -p ~/.openclaw/{venvs,models,logs,completions}
dd if=/dev/zero of=~/.openclaw/venvs/large-file bs=1M count=100

# 2. Backup WITHOUT smart-exclude — includes everything
openclaw backup create --output /tmp/backups --dry-run --json
# → venvs/large-file appears in assets

# 3. Backup WITH smart-exclude — excludes bulky dirs
openclaw backup create --smart-exclude --output /tmp/backups --dry-run --json
# → venvs/ excluded, shown in excludedStats

# 4. Full backup with smart-exclude
openclaw backup create --smart-exclude --output /tmp/backups --verify
# → Completes successfully, verify passes

# 5. Custom exclude pattern
openclaw backup create --exclude "*.log" --exclude "**/__pycache__" --output /tmp/backups

# 6. Protected path guard (exact match)
openclaw backup create --exclude "credentials/" --output /tmp/backups
# → Warning: pattern matches protected path

# 7. Protected path guard (glob match — also caught)
openclaw backup create --exclude "cred*" --output /tmp/backups
# → Warning: pattern "cred*" would match protected path "credentials/"

# 8. Override protected path guard
openclaw backup create --exclude "credentials/" --allow-exclude-protected --output /tmp/backups
```

## Evidence

- [x] Unit tests passing (500 lines, 42 tests)
- [x] Integration tests passing (562 lines, 15 tests)
- [x] CLI forwarding tests passing (159 lines, 10 tests)
- [x] Verify command handles archives with and without `excludedStats`
- [x] Backward compatible with existing archives (missing `excludedStats` field = normal verify)
- [x] 67 backup-related tests passing; full suite pre-existing failures unrelated to this PR

## Human Verification

- Tested with real 11GB `.openclaw` state directory containing `venvs/` (4.2GB), `models/` (3.1GB), `logs/` (2.6GB)
- `--smart-exclude` reduces backup from 11GB bloated archive → focused 380MB archive in ~45 seconds
- Verified `openclaw backup verify` passes on archives created with exclude patterns
- Verified archives created without exclude flags are identical to pre-patch behavior

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? **Yes** — no behavior change when no exclude flags are passed
- Config/env changes? **No**
- Migration needed? **No**
- Existing archives verify correctly with updated verify command

## Risks and Mitigations

| Risk                                                          | Mitigation                                                                                                                        |
| ------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Larger PR (+2440 lines)                                       | 45% is test code (1,221 lines); dedicated `src/infra/backup-exclude.ts` keeps production logic focused                            |
| One new dependency (`ignore`)                                 | 28M weekly downloads, zero known vulnerabilities; `minimatch` is already in the codebase but lacks full gitignore spec compliance |
| `--smart-exclude` defaults might exclude something users want | Opt-in flag; `--include-all` provides escape hatch; manifest records what was excluded                                            |
| Two-step archive adds complexity                              | Only activated when excludes are present; simple path unchanged                                                                   |

## AI Disclosure

This implementation was AI-assisted (Claude Code). All code was reviewed by a human. Architecture, safety model, and flag design were human-directed.

**AI review process:** Used Claude Code multi-agent review workflow (ce:review) in lieu of `codex review` — 6 specialist agents (TypeScript, security, performance, architecture, simplicity, agent-native) identified 18 findings across P1/P2/P3 severity levels. All 18 findings were resolved prior to this PR. This is equivalent to or more thorough than a single `codex review` pass.

target.title: Backup: add encrypted snapshot backup flow
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: OpenClaw had local backup export/verify, but no low-friction folder-backed snapshot flow for encrypted backups plus workspace mirroring.
- Why it matters: users need a simple `backup setup/run/status/list/restore` flow for day-to-day protection and full-host recovery without syncing the live state dir.
- What changed: added folder-backed encrypted snapshot storage, workspace backup setup/run/status, restore/list plumbing, config schema/docs, and the follow-up hardening/regression coverage that addressed the P1/P2 review feedback from #41345.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this does not add provider-specific cloud APIs, device-identity export/import, or any live state-dir sync behavior.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [x] Refactor
- [x] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related #32921
- Related #40090
- Related #7598
- Related #41345

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Added `openclaw backup setup`, `openclaw backup run`, `openclaw backup status`, `openclaw backup list`, and `openclaw backup restore`.
- `openclaw backup export` is now the primary local archive command; `backup create` stays as a deprecated alias.
- `backup push` stays as a deprecated alias for snapshot creation through `backup run`.
- `reset` and `uninstall` now recommend `openclaw backup export`.
- Backup config now supports `backup.target` and `backup.encryption.key` for folder-backed encrypted snapshots.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:
  - Backup now writes encrypted snapshots and mirrored workspaces into a user-selected backup target. Mitigations: target validation rejects unsafe paths, workspace mirroring rejects symlinked trees, restore validates envelope crypto metadata before decrypt, and restore writes now create/copy through safe parent paths to avoid symlink races into protected directories.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 22 / pnpm workspace
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A
- Relevant config (redacted): `backup.target=<temp dir>`, `backup.encryption.key=<redacted>`

### Steps

1. Run the backup-focused Vitest suite covering snapshot storage, CLI wiring, backup run/list/restore, workspace mirroring, and reset/uninstall integration.
2. Run `pnpm build` and `pnpm check` on the rebased branch.
3. Attempt a local CLI smoke (`pnpm openclaw backup run/list/restore`) with a temp HOME and snapshot config.

### Expected

- Backup commands create/list/restore encrypted snapshots and mirror workspaces safely.
- Build/check pass unless blocked by unrelated main-branch issues.
- Local CLI smoke exercises the new flow end-to-end.

### Actual

- The backup-focused Vitest suite passed.
- `pnpm build`, `pnpm check`, and the local CLI smoke are currently blocked by unrelated `main` breakage after rebasing: `@mariozechner/pi-ai` no longer exports `getOAuthApiKey`, `getOAuthProviders`, or `loginOpenAICodex`, which breaks `src/agents/auth-profiles/oauth.ts`, `src/commands/openai-codex-oauth.ts`, and several agent wrapper files before backup code runs.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Commands run:

```bash
pnpm exec vitest run src/commands/backup.test.ts src/commands/backup-run.test.ts src/commands/backup-verify.test.ts src/commands/backup-status.test.ts src/commands/backup-snapshot.test.ts src/commands/backup-restore.test.ts src/commands/workspace-backup.test.ts src/backup/snapshot-store/config.test.ts src/backup/snapshot-store/encryption.test.ts src/backup/snapshot-store/provider-folder.test.ts src/backup/snapshot-store/targets.test.ts src/cli/program/register.backup.test.ts src/cli/program/command-registry.test.ts
pnpm build
pnpm check
HOME=<temp-home> pnpm openclaw backup run --json
HOME=<temp-home> pnpm openclaw backup list --json
HOME=<temp-home> pnpm openclaw backup restore <snapshotId> --mode workspace-only --json
```

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: backup-focused test suite on the rebased branch, including snapshot create/list/restore, workspace backup setup/run/status, restore mapping, rollback handling, JSON output, and the added envelope/symlink hardening regressions.
- Edge cases checked: malformed envelope crypto metadata rejection, symlink rejection during workspace mirroring, restore refusal when a missing parent path becomes a symlink, deprecated alias behavior, listing snapshots without an encryption key, and external config/oauth restore paths.
- What you did **not** verify: a successful local CLI smoke after rebase, because current `main` cannot boot the CLI due the unrelated `pi-ai` OAuth export mismatch described above.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:
  - Existing users can keep using `backup create` / `backup push`, but the preferred commands are `backup export` / `backup run`.
  - To enable encrypted snapshots, set `backup.target` and `backup.encryption.key`.

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: keep using `openclaw backup export` for local archives only, or revert commit `3ecc067b4`.
- Files/config to restore: `openclaw.json` backup section, snapshot files under the selected backup target, and any scripts that still use `backup create` / `backup push`.
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: `backup run` falling back to workspace mirroring when snapshot config is incomplete, restore rejecting unsafe target paths, and current unrelated `main` CLI startup failures in OAuth agent wiring.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: backup target misconfiguration could point into unsafe or recursive paths.
  - Mitigation: target validation rejects state dir, workspace, OAuth, and backup-workspace-root overlaps before writes.
- Risk: malformed snapshot envelopes could consume excessive resources or leave restore paths unsafe.
  - Mitigation: envelope crypto metadata is range/shape validated before decrypt, and restore now creates/copies through safe parent paths instead of trusting nonexistent path suffixes.
- Risk: alias compatibility could drift from the primary commands.
  - Mitigation: dedicated CLI and command tests cover `backup create`, `backup push`, `backup export`, `backup run`, and `backup restore` behavior.

target.title: fix(claude-live): return updatedInput instead of deprecated behavior:allow for can_use_tool
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

OpenClaw 2026.6.8 is incompatible with `@anthropic-ai/claude-code@2.1.156`. Every tool call that goes through the permission/approval flow fails with a `ZodError` before the tool actually runs.

Claude Code 2.1.x tightened the `PermissionResult` schema. The new union accepts:
- `{ updatedInput: <record> }` — allow (with possibly-modified inputs)
- `{ behavior: "deny", message: <string> }` — deny with reason

OpenClaw's bridge was returning the older shape `{ behavior: "allow" }` (no `updatedInput`) in **two separate code paths**, which validates against neither branch and causes ZodError in both the Claude live-session flow and the native hook relay flow.

**Fixes both allow-response paths identified by the ClawSweeper review.**

## Evidence

### Fix 1 — Claude live-session path (`claude-live-session.ts:877`)

```diff
   response: allowed
     ? {
-        behavior: "allow",
+        updatedInput: input ?? {},
         ...(toolUseId ? { toolUseID: toolUseId } : {}),
       }
```

### Fix 2 — Native hook relay path (`native-hook-relay.ts:396`)

The codex adapter's `renderPermissionDecisionResponse` now returns `{ updatedInput: input }` for allow decisions, and `runNativeHookRelayPermissionRequest` passes `request.toolInput` to all three allow call sites:

```diff
-    renderPermissionDecisionResponse: (decision, message) => ({
+    renderPermissionDecisionResponse: (decision, message, input) => ({
       ...
       decision:
         decision === "allow"
-          ? { behavior: "allow" }
+          ? { updatedInput: input ?? {} }
```

All three allow call sites (allow-always cache hit, allow decision, allow-always new) now pass `request.toolInput` as the third argument.

### Test coverage

**cli-runner.spawn.test.ts** — 3 allow-path tests updated:

| Test case | Assertion |
|-----------|----------|
| exec policy full/no-ask | `updatedInput` equals `{ command: "ls" }` |
| no exec policy (default) | `updatedInput` equals `{ command: "echo hi" }` |
| YOLO bypassPermissions | `updatedInput` equals `{ command: "ls" }` |

**native-hook-relay.test.ts** — 8 allow-path assertions updated from `toEqual({ behavior: "allow" })` to `toMatchObject({ updatedInput: expect.any(Object) })`:

| Test | Assertion |
|------|----------|
| duplicate permission allow-always | `updatedInput: expect.any(Object)` |
| primary permission allow | `updatedInput: expect.any(Object)` |
| allow-always remembered | `updatedInput: expect.any(Object)` |
| deny path (unchanged) | `behavior: "deny"` |

All deny-path tests remain unchanged — Claude Code 2.1.x still accepts the `{ behavior: "deny", message }` branch.

### Files changed

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/agents/cli-runner/claude-live-session.ts` | replace `behavior: "allow"` with `updatedInput: input` |
| `src/agents/cli-runner.spawn.test.ts` | 3 assertions: `behavior` → `updatedInput` |

target.title: fix: correct loopback address detection across multiple subsystems
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Three loopback/private address validation bugs across different subsystems, all following the same pattern: address ranges that are incomplete or semantically wrong.

1. **diffs `isLoopbackClientIp`**: Only matches exact `127.0.0.1`, missing the full 127.0.0.0/8 loopback range. Container and sandbox environments commonly use other addresses like `127.0.0.2`.

2. **audit-extra `isLoopbackPublishHost`**: Same pattern — only matches exact `127.0.0.1` instead of the full 127.0.0.0/8 range.

3. **device-pair `isLoopbackHost`**: Treats `0.0.0.0` (INADDR_ANY — bind to all interfaces) and `::` (IPv6 unspecified) as loopback. These are NOT loopback addresses. The canonical implementation in `src/gateway/net.ts` explicitly excludes both.

## Why This Change Was Made

Each fix is minimal (1-2 lines) and follows the established patterns from the canonical implementations in `src/gateway/net.ts` and `packages/net-policy/src/ip.ts`.

**Intentionally NOT changed:**
- `model-auth isLocalBaseUrl()`: `0.0.0.0` is preserved because existing tests and sibling code (`llm-idle-timeout.ts`) treat it as a local provider endpoint for synthetic local auth. This is an established contract.
- `session-manager` loop bounds: The `<=` in `buildIndex()` and `getPersistedFileEntries()` is intentional — `rememberLeafControl()` anchors opaque records at `fileEntries.length`, and the tail pass is required to read them back.

## Evidence

- **diffs tests**: 10 test files, 135 tests all pass
- **audit-extra tests**: 6/6 pass (including `isLoopbackPublishHost` coverage)
- **net.test.ts `isLoopbackHost`**: 49 test cases pass, explicitly verifying `0.0.0.0` and `::` are NOT loopback in the canonical implementation

target.title: fix(auto-reply): use a chat_type-neutral group/channel intro opener
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Resolves #95645.

`buildGroupChatContext` is built for **both** `group` and `channel` ChatTypes (the caller gates on `isGroupChat = ChatType === "group" || ChatType === "channel"` in `get-reply-run`), but its opener hard-coded:

```
You are in a <provider> group chat.
```

For a **public channel** the authoritative inbound-meta block reports `chat_type: "channel"`, while `"group"` denotes a private channel / group DM. So the natural-language opener contradicts the trusted metadata, and "group chat" is the more salient signal. Agents key off it and emit `chat_type: group` (or `--chat-type group` to tools) for a public channel.

Because session keys are namespaced `…:mattermost:<direct|channel|group>:<peerId>:thread:<root>`, a wrong `group` for a public channel addresses a **non-existent** session and breaks downstream routing.

## Fix

Reword the opener so it no longer declares a `chat_type`:

```diff
-  lines.push(`You are in a ${providerLabel} group chat.`);
+  lines.push(`You are in a shared ${providerLabel} chat (not a 1:1 DM).`);
```

This is the neutral wording proposed in the issue. It is **provider-agnostic** — `buildGroupChatContext` is shared by all providers, and "shared chat (not a 1:1 DM)" holds for every non-DM conversation this intro fires for. The remaining `"group"` mentions in the function ("be a good group participant", "…sent to this group chat") are colloquial role/delivery guidance, not a type declaration, so they are intentionally left unchanged.

## Scope

Deliberately narrow: this is the system-prompt-intro half only. The deeper `chat_type`-derivation inconsistency for private channels (channel-type vs target-string inference) is tracked separately in #95646 and is **not** addressed here.

## What changed

- `src/auto-reply/reply/groups.ts` — neutral opener + rationale comment.
- `src/auto-reply/reply/groups.test.ts` — updated expectation; **new regression test** asserting a public-channel (`ChatType: "channel"`) context opens neutrally and never says "… group chat".
- `src/auto-reply/reply.triggers.group-intro-prompts.cases.ts` — updated the Discord/WhatsApp/Telegram opener expectations (delivery-guidance text unchanged).
- `src/agents/prompt-composition.test.ts` — updated the Slack group opener invariant.
- `test/fixtures/agents/prompt-snapshots/codex-runtime-happy-path/discord-group-codex-message-tool.md` — regenerated via `pnpm prompt:snapshots:gen` (the opener change shifts the recorded char/token counts).

## Evidence (after-fix, on a live deployment)

The one-line opener reword is running in production (applied over the deployed `dist/get-reply-*.js`).

**1. Deployed opener — verbatim from the running container.** The agent declines to quote its system prompt verbatim, so the rendered template is shown directly from `dist` (`${providerLabel}` is substituted at runtime, e.g. `Mattermost`); the old type-declaring opener is gone:

```console
$ docker exec <gateway> sh -c "grep -rho 'You are in a shared.*1:1 DM)' /app/dist/get-reply-*.js | sort -u"
You are in a shared ${providerLabel} chat (not a 1:1 DM).

$ docker exec <gateway> sh -c "grep -hoc 'You are in a \${providerLabel} group chat' /app/dist/get-reply-*.js"
0
```

**2. Public channel → `chat_type: channel`.** The agent reads the trusted `chat_type` as `channel` and frames the venue as a shared chat that is *not a 1:1 DM* — no "group chat" type-word:

![public channel turn](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/iloveleon19/openclaw/pr-proof-assets/proof-96069-channel.png)

> **Q:** report (1) the `chat_type` in your Inbound Context (trusted metadata), and (2) the opener describing this venue.
> **A:** (1) `chat_type` is `channel`. (2) *(declines to quote verbatim)* … this is a shared Mattermost chat, not a 1:1 DM.

**3. Private channel → `chat_type: group`.** Same neutral opener; the agent reads the trusted `chat_type` as `group`. Confirms the opener no longer forces "group" wording and the type comes from trusted metadata:

![private channel turn](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/iloveleon19/openclaw/pr-proof-assets/proof-96069-group.png)

> **A:** (1) `chat_type` is `group`. (2) *(declines to quote verbatim)* … this is a shared Mattermost chat, not a 1:1 DM.

Before the fix the opener read `You are in a <provider> group chat.` for **both** of the above — which (per #95645) led agents to emit `chat_type: group` for a public channel, binding a non-existent `group:<channelId>` session and breaking threaded delivery.

**CI:** the Codex prompt snapshot was regenerated; `prompt:snapshots:check` and the auto-reply / prompt-composition tests pass on the current head.
